So what exactly is going on here? No one's sure, but what's known is that the Rare Enchantling's skill in Monstromancy is just as strong as its cousin's. Drawing energy from the unrefined crystals that dangle to and fro, screaming forth its captivating cry: a must-see in any tour of the pocket dimension.
data.3123596607:
The Hairionette's existence is defined by cruel irony: despite its status as the Control Elemental, this Paironormal is convinced that its destiny is not its own to choose. Hairionette firmly believes that its every move is predetermined, pulled along its life path by the skeins of fate. It is driven by its overwhelming need to find just who is pulling the strings, never pausing to wonder whether it is in fact the mastermind behind its own designs. So just who is the true puppetmaster: a mysterious manipulator, or the hapless Hairionette itself? This Paironormal's Major and Minor forms reflect both existential options.

This Monster, like all Core Paironormal Monsters, has both a Major and Minor form, depending on the island on which it appears. These dual forms have alternating availability over the course of the day, so you'll need to check which one is available as you try to add it to your collection!

data.1018838929:
The Hoax Paironormal lives by the four D's: deceive, dissemble, dissimulate and distract! Owlesque's Major and Minor forms symbolize the false projection of ourselves that we present to others, and the true self that we keep concealed. Yet, neither form is any less lovely than the other: with a simple flip, both aspects of Owlesque are equally striking, offering an unsettlingly strigine elegance and eye-catching markings (if you know which eye to catch). For all its supposed trickery, this Paironormal's existence encourages radical acceptance of all aspects of ourselves, and its piercing eyes (both camouflaged and corporeal) dare you to believe that beauty is simply a matter of perspective.

This Monster, like all Core Paironormal Monsters, has both a Major and Minor form, depending on the island on which it appears. These dual forms have alternating availability over the course of the day, so you'll need to check which one is available as you try to add it to your collection!

data.4159106100:
Arcorina is representative of the Ruin Element... but just like the Paironormal class itself, there is a duality and ambiguity to the interpretation of this Element's name. You see, most Monster scholars take it at face value and believe that 'Ruin' refers to a slow-moving process - namely, the pristine perfection of Arcorina's Major form gradually falling victim to erosion and the inexorable march of time, ultimately dilapidating into the Minor. A much smaller group of academics contends that this is a case of linguistic homophony; that 'Rune' is the superior discernment of Arcorina's Element, given the cryptic markings that flash across the bodies of both forms as they play. What do they, in turn, represent? Those same academics admit they haven't the foggiest. For now, the Ruin/Rune debate skews to the former, which is at least supported by Arcorina's own architectural resonance - it's quite often mistaken for a Structure, even as its many hands perform their hypnotic dance.

This Monster, like all Core Paironormal Monsters, has both a Major and Minor form, depending on the island on which it appears. These dual forms have alternating availability over the course of the day, so you'll need to check which one is available as you try to add it to your collection!

data.3940801676:
There is a commonly held misconception that the depths of the Beyond are unfathomably dark. The great, uncharted abyss beyond the Monster World may yet contain vast expanses of undiscovered cosmic mysteries to the likes of Monster and human alike, but as with all scientific endeavors, the truth is limited to our perception and understanding. The Depths are in fact alight with gleaming radiance, though nigh imperceptible across astronomical distances. Gleaming in the darkness is the furtive Shhimmer, a Paironormal whose Major and Minor forms evoke the dark, unknowable void of space and the impenetrable profundity of deep water respectively. Its prominently inquisitive eye peers back across the expanse, employing a rotating series of hexagonal waveforms to pierce the boundary between worlds. The Shhimmer's incredibly complex eye boasts a novel form of polarization, granting it the ability to perceive not just light beyond the normal visible spectrum, but to flip between dimensional perspectives. Ironically, its monocular vision means that it lacks Depth perception.

This Monster, like all Core Paironormal Monsters, has both a Major and Minor form, depending on the island on which it appears. These dual forms have alternating availability over the course of the day, so you'll need to check which one is available as you try to add it to your collection!

data.566169385:
Featuring bbno$

Scallywag? Rapscallion? No... Mon $caggs? Oh, Scallyrags you rascal, why is your name so hard to get right? The Rascal Paironormal is an enigma wrapped in a riddle, wrapped in a vest... or a trenchcoat. Actually, it seems to be three distinct creatures stacked on top of each other? What's important is that this Big Top-hatted Monster can be big time trouble for its fellow Paironormals. A Mon of many faces, Scallyrags is a masterful manipulator able to influence precisely what kind of impression it makes on those around it. Some describe themselves curiously drawn to the dapper Monster upon first encountering it, bemused and delighted by its dainty movements. Others meanwhile experience an immediate and almost primal aversion to the Monster, wary of its eerily unsettling nature. Of course, in its Minor form, it becomes far clearer that the youthful creatures (who lack distinct names of their own) are motivated to deceive and manipulate borne of a complaint that they don't have a big enough allowance. Indeed, they could be said to be "babies with no money." How exactly the rest of the Monsters on Paironormal Carnival haven't yet gotten wise to their schemes is anyone's guess. One thing is certain, you will only see the Scallyrags that Scallyrags wants you to see.

On Paironormal Carnival, this Monster ignores the day/night restrictions of its parent class and is available to collect at all times.
data.1819782690:
Each sentient being has its own 'inner world' - a place where it can retreat from the pressures of daily life. In the case of eRmA gUrDy - yes, that's its full name - no, the mixed case spelling is not negotiable - uh, where were we? Right - in the case of eRmA gUrDy, the Mutual Paironormal, 'inner world' takes on a completely different meaning. The Monster's entire body is made up of a viscous, organic substance that occurs naturally in the deepest parts of the Beyond, and which serves as a vivarium for other lifeforms from even deeper parts. In its Major 'eRmA' form, the five-limbed body shelters the smaller lifeforms, and in exchange, they facilitate the performance of delightful music by swirling around in the substance. A perfect example of selfless mutualism, with neither side in control of the other... but things aren't quite as rosy in its 'gUrDy' form. The environmental quirks of the Minor Carnival cause the substance to undergo a chemical transformation that dissolves its eyes, and suddenly the Monster decides to commandeer the living arrangement. In response, the lifeforms mount a defense and secrete hard 'teeth' that they use to try and burrow out of the substance. Ultimately, gUrDy gathers up the 'teeth' and strives to continue its musical performance, and the lifeforms begrudgingly act as the Monster's 'eyes' to navigate the walkways of the Carnival.

On Paironormal Carnival, this Monster ignores the day/night restrictions of its parent class and is available to collect at all times.
data.2636193015:
Hey I know you! The frenetically flailing flurry of bombastic energy from this enneapod is positively infectious! But one look at the jaunty gesticulations of Dakktyl, and you'll start to have a hard time telling up from down. You'd think the fate of the Monster World depended on it with the amount of gusto the Bombast Paironormal puts into its performance, but it's actually essential for the Monster to maintain its form. Dakktyl is constantly one pop or lock away from collapsing, and is paradoxically able to maintain perfect stability through complete chaotic disorder. Perhaps it is thanks to its unconventional symmetry, or the fact that the Monster's undeniable amount of sass is somehow present in both its Major and Minor forms. However, the real question is whether or not the Dakktyl knowingly participates in such strenuous physical activity when its energy is going to run out eventually? The toll is certainly visible in the ruined posture and flexibility of its Minor manifestation. Sometimes it's possible that the clanging and banging of its inner parts can even cause a state of self-inflected amnesia for Dakktyl, and that it must start every new day with a freshly-wiped memory, but... ¡noʎ ʍouʞ I ʎǝH

On Paironormal Carnival, this Monster ignores the day/night restrictions of its parent class and is available to collect at all times.
data.475612811:
Each morning, the Jerm reflects on the passage of time... when perfectly on cue, something flits by its ear to remind it that it's not alone. It's never alone, in fact. Ever. The Cycle Paironormal exists in a state of constant genesis, effluxion, consumption, and regenesis. The fruiting bodies pocking the abdomen of Jerm gradually sap vitality from the main body, enervating the Monster, before expelling themselves in a flourish of whirlygigs. This process, while quite unusual, is a natural part of Jerm's lifecycle - and it's a delightful sonic experience to witness! These bodily expulsions germinate, grow, and eventually re-incorporate, or subsume the original Jerm body, forming the Monster anew. Jerm's auto-cultivation serves to harmonize the Monster's form, spirit, fortune and misfortune. Eventually, an ambitious excrescence will latch onto the Jerm's body, robbing it of the control it once had to fly freely. Cycles this Paironormal must continue to undergo in its Minor form lead to its despondent expression, probably due to the fairly uncomfortable process of pushing out soggier fruiting bodies in an ever-hardening abdomen and all over its underside where the 'tree' is attached. The circle of life can be beautiful, and sometimes very, very gloopy.

On Paironormal Carnival, this Monster ignores the day/night restrictions of its parent class and is available to collect at all times.
data.2597139493:
Monstrological taxonomy has been forced to reimagine many well-established methods of classification, thanks in large part to the often confounding nature of the Paironormals. Pareidolia is at play when beholding the Galymph, whose Major form presents as a pair of Monsters, or... a pair of a pair. A pair of Paironormals. With its obscuring frills, calcified cladding and matted aetherfronds, the Ambiguity Paironormal obfuscates its true form with a subtle deftness. The sound of its voices singing in unison suggests a cooperation between two beings - with the 'rider' mindfully controlling the 'steed' - but one must be cautious they aren’t being taken for a ride. When revealed in its Minor form, the dorsal cladding has ablated, revealing a body pocked with plasmonodules. Its aetherfronds, now more sparse, have lost their lushness, exposing that the 'steed and rider' combo is, in fact, a single connected Monster. Observers of both Major and Minor Galymphs share a preternatural understanding of a mysterious truth: that this Paironormal is actually the micro-organism dwelling in the fluid-filled vessels of a gargantuan creature, under the sway of this mysterious force from beyond the Carnival.

On Paironormal Carnival, this Monster ignores the day/night restrictions of its parent class and is available to collect at all times.
data.1284822840:
The Mesmer Paironormal Illoost is a Monster out of time. Its attitude and outlook being somewhat of an anachronism, this Triple-Element Paironormal lives out its days with an "as if" attitude. Rather than seeing its hodge-podge of parts as a mismatched amalgamation, this Carnival resident considers every peculiar bit removed, every colorful motif embellished, and every gangly doohickey added as steps towards becoming who it really wants to be, "as if" it was always destined to turn out this way. When the Illoost was first spotted on the Carnival, conflicting reports of its initial appearance gave pause. Descriptions varied wildly, leading different Paironormal researchers to form diverse conclusions. Some depicted an inviting figure replete with fanciful patterns, sounds, colors, and impossible optical perceptions to dazzle and enlighten. Others, by contrast, painted the picture of a discombobulated creature straining for attention, sputtering its song and daring its fellow Carnival revelers to behold its mystifying optical tricks. Did the Illoost not always look like this? Do these descriptions simply illustrate the dual Major and Minor quality of its Paironormal class? Or is it possible that every being that looks upon the Monster sees something unique? To the Illoost, there are no illusions in its abstractions, and the chaos of its form reveals its true nature. Identity is change, and a mask can become truth.... as if!

On Paironormal Carnival, this Monster ignores the day/night restrictions of its parent class and is available to collect at all times.
data.2952846317:
This strange object defies conventional classification. Grown from a humble seedpod delivered from Beyond, it at once resembles a gigantic flower bud that has yet to bloom, and a chrysalis inside which... well, no one quite knows what's in there. It certainly looks like it belongs on this Magical Island - its color scheme and aesthetic make it look right at home among the rocks, trees and landscape of the resident Titan. An energy signature that is equal parts recognizable and alien seems to emanate from the bud, calling out to the diverse Monsters who observe from a distance and endlessly speculate about what it could be. There's no doubt that it's captured the 'soul' of the island, but perhaps in this case, that expression is more than figurative...

Gather some intrepid Monster volunteers and see what happens when you establish a 'Soul Link' with this strange object!
data.936981800: Stirred by the Monster denizens of Psychic Island, the Cruv'laaphtian Crocus has arisen! The distinction between 'flower' and Monster is blurred ever further in the physiology of this Titansoul - it's as if they have been fused into one entity with little sense of where one ends the other begins. It's unmistakably connected to the soul of the Psychic Titan, though - writhing tentacles reconstituted as roots and claws alike, brain matter coursing with synaptic action, and critter-like eyes it manipulates like juggling balls! One gets the ominous sense during its performance 'conducting' the Island's music that it is straining to pull itself out of the ground and float upwards into the endless Beyond. Perhaps its musical manipulation of the larger eyes, tentacle electrocutions and streaking meteor showers represent its attempts to destabilize the island and dislodge itself from the ground. Alas, Cruv'laapht is far too clever for that. Even though it will likely never tire of its ambition to break free and explore the cosmos, the Crocus is content to embody the musical aspirations of Cruv'laapht, and cements the Titan's place in the Monster World.

data.2974631814: Stirred by the Monster denizens of Faerie Island, the Faesoddoid Fungus has arisen! We know what you're thinking - aren't Titansouls meant to evoke flower-like forms? That's what leading researchers believed as well, until the arrival of the Fungus expanded understanding of these classification-defying organisms and redefined them as more generalized 'fruiting bodies.' Since the Faesodd Titan is so strongly connected to mushrooms, it should come as no surprise that its 'flower' more closely resembles a Fungus! This gentle being rises above Faesodd's self-consciousness and reveals the Titan's inner beauty and sensitivity through its fluid movements and confident conducting. The Titansoul's magic is present across the landscape, inspiring the diminutive spirit critters to bound around the Faerie ring and prompting Faesodd to spout fantastical, rainbow-colored steam from its ears. Even the Titan's mask responds to the Faesoddoid Fungus's fan flourishes, emitting light and sound from the tips of its gleaming bulbs. Creative self-expression is essential in a balanced existence, and the music Faesodd is able to achieve through its Titansoul cements the Titan's place in the Monster World.

data.2336696054: Stirred by the Monster denizens of Light Island, the Phosphoran Phlox has arisen! Is this entity the flower or the Monster? In short, it's both... and it's neither. The true nature of the Titansoul is complex: it's a physical manifestation of the 'soul' of the Phosphora Titan, but it also appears to have a life-force distinct from its Titanic host. Light Island Monsters play a vital role in its inspiriting, conferring Soul'er Power by 'Linking' to the Titansoul and progressively raising its power level. The Phosphoran Phlox itself is silent, but as its Soul'er Power grows, it is able to exercise astounding musical control over its environment: manipulating the Aurora Monstralis overhead, conjuring dazzling light effects along the Titan's brow, and transforming nodules into musical lamps! Phosphora's desire to join in the song is no doubt inspired by its mentor's admiration of the Colossals' musical gifts, and the Phosphoran Phlox helps to cement the Titan's place in the Monster World.
data.2280154753:
The following is an excerpt retrieved from the Monster development journals of an unidentified Colossal, chronicling some of their earliest recorded efforts in the pocket dimension:
"Progress halted on the skittish specimen codenamed 'Lowb.' Subject initially showed encouraging signs of maturation and musical resonance, but the duration of these fails to meet performance targets. Internal structure of the subject's thoracic cavity was designed to house a flowering bud, but external stimuli cause an unanticipated hardening of the outer shell. As such, this model has been shelved. Disappointing news, as the hope was for this creation to be designated as the Core Elemental of Plant. Nevertheless, the work continues: a promising new design has been stabilized with the assistance of pottery and will be explored further."
Seemingly added much later, another Colossal's notes have been scribbled in the margin:
"Cancellation of the Lowb prototype was premature. Using the Dish-Harmonizer, I was able to harness the subject's shell hardening ability to fashion a dendritic structure composed of elder specimens on which the juvenile specimen can bounce. A constant cycle of renewal is now underway, in which old becomes reabsorbed and born anew. I knew this idea had potential, it just needed a few tweaks: now the Plant Primordial has a new lease on life."
data.24908847:
The following is an excerpt retrieved from the Monster development journals of an unidentified Colossal, chronicling some of their earliest recorded efforts in the pocket dimension:
"Acts of 'Fandhul'-ism (what we're calling work on the Core Cold Elemental) have been put on ice (couldn't resist). The team agrees that the final design is within reach - attributes like a furry body and oversized hands/feet are popular - but we've reached an impasse when it comes to stabilizing those very hands. Mammoth mitts are needed for optimal snowball fight prowess, but their heftiness necessitates outfitting the Monster's arms with robotic sleeves. "Z" has no objection (they love all that), but it doesn't match the greater vision. Although "Z" still insists that a hexapedal body plan for a Cold Elemental would be, and I quote, "Epic," proposed direction is to simplify greatly and pare back the number of limbs."
Seemingly added much later, another Colossal's notes have been scribbled in the margin:
"Perusing the initial notes, the expression 'within reach' was the inspiration for Fandhul's reimagined form. Why struggle with reinforcing arms to support hands when you can rework the hands entirely? Replicating the Colossal ability to project parts of consciousness into hand form, the updated Fandhul eschews arms entirely and simply crafts cryokinetic extensions of itself that can clap and rub to make its music! When the Cold Primordial needs to bulk up its snowy paws, it need only emit frozen breath. The Fandhul's innate craftiness has only been augmented now that it has no fingerprints, but fortunately, it seems less fixated on underhanded deeds and more on... fantasy sports?"
data.3391145866:
The following is an excerpt retrieved from the Monster development journals of an unidentified Colossal, chronicling some of their earliest recorded efforts in the pocket dimension:
"The 'Bogle' project has unfortunately been placed on indefinite hiatus. Thought we were really onto something, designing a creature that stored air within a solid globe on its back to produce its music. That would be its air supply as it sang forth. Alas, the plans for the Core Air Elemental didn't really come together the way we planned - the inflexibility of the globe makes inhalation and exhalation a challenge for Bogle, coupled with its intense weight requiring multiple legs slapdashedly added to support it. We're starting from scratch on this one, although we'll be keeping a few aspects - it'll still be a vocalist, and we're all quite partial to the color scheme."
Seemingly added much later, another Colossal's notes have been scribbled in the margin:
"A fresh perspective on Bogle has rendered this Monster viable. Most importantly - no longer a vocalist. The rigid globe of the original has been swapped out for an elastic air sac. Now that that weight has been lifted, the load-bearing legs have been converted into tubes through which the air can flow. Preserved the original's distracted personality because it's charming, but added a few avian touches of the final Air Elemental (like furthers and claws) for good measure."
data.3609589554:
The following is an excerpt retrieved from the Monster development journals of an unidentified Colossal, chronicling some of their earliest recorded efforts in the pocket dimension:
"Advancement of the Droah prototype at a standstill. Unlike other Core Elemental initiatives, however, not a result of the design being non-viable. Instead, we're divided on which of the Elements the Droah most embodies. My personal inclination, of course, is Water; its columnar body and tentacle-covered head call to mind the deep-sea Anemonon. G, on the other hand, lays claim for the Plant Element, citing the fact that it grows from a rooted base, although their proposal would see that part covered by some kind of crockery. Tried to convince the assembly that its rows of teeth would be best-suited to a fearsome sea creature, but they seem to leaning towards Plant. Debate got so heated at one point that R had to intervene to mediate. Haven't given up just yet, though. Something about the notion of a rubbery spheroid with multiple stubby bits jutting out that I can't get out of my head - it's really jammed in there."
Seemingly added much later, another Colossal's notes have been scribbled in the margin:
"Revisiting the old design required less revision than some of the others, so took on additional challenges to put a personal spin on Droah. Sourced some of H's adventuring journals and became inspired by mentions of a Leafy Sea Dragon observed in a journey to a distant world; converted some of the stubby tentacles into branched lobes to differentiate further from the final Core Water Elemental. Lost the teeth entirely (that trait ended up becoming a hallmark of the Core Plant Elemental anyways) and instead... actually, going to let the change 'speak' for itself."

data.1095783954: Monster eggs must incubate. Their quirky chemistry means that some will hatch quickly while others need a long time. The Nursery can accommodate only a single egg, but it will receive the tenderest of pampering, thanks to a soft bedding of hay and just the right amount of warmth from the lamp.
data.3351520444: Cutting-edge advances in Monster-Nursology have culminated in this brand new Structure upgrade! The Enhanced Nursery features a warmer lamp, gnarlier toadstools, and shorter incubation times... 25% shorter, that is!
data.211351321:
It's especially advantageous to have a Bonus Nursery on hand when seeking special Monsters to add to your collection.

The Monster-Handlers would like to extend a heartfelt thank-you for your support of My Singing Monsters! They couldn't do it without you!
data.1577321995: Monster eggs must incubate. Their quirky chemistry means that some will hatch quickly while others need a long time. The Nursery can accommodate only a single egg, but it will receive the tenderest of pampering, thanks to a soft bedding of hay and just the right amount of warmth from the lamp.
data.4229306461: Cutting-edge advances in Monster-Nursology have culminated in this brand new Structure upgrade! The Enhanced Nursery features a warmer lamp, gnarlier toadstools, and shorter incubation times... 25% shorter, that is!
data.3327706519: If it looks like a Nursery... and smells like a Nursery... and tastes like a Nursery (blech)... it must be a Nursery! In the variable temperatures of the Beyond, a heatlamp can provide the much-needed warmth to make sure that eggs incubate properly.
data.3609571310: Just because you've added a bunch of gold highlights to something doesn't mean that it's automatically better. But in the case of the Enhanced "Nursery" on Paironormal Carnival, that's exactly what it means - shorter incubation times by 25%!
data.2570178847: This peculiar structure has been in use since the golden age of the Monster World, and little has changed since that time. Once it is old enough, a Monster can spontaneously produce an egg containing all of its genetic and elemental information, which is then placed onto one of the 'bowls' of the machine. These eggs, which are truly more like spores, slowly seep out their 'precious bodily fluids' down the twin drains of the structure and magically co-mingle in the center vessel, until a new egg is produced! The process is complicated though, as one breeding combination can have many possible outcomes.
data.2146665677: Breeding is tricky business at the best of times, so every little advantage helps. Thanks to the invaluable field research that has been collected over the past few years, the complex interplay of Elemental powers is now better understood than ever before! This Enhanced Breeding Structure cuts breeding wait times by 25%!
data.3031390056:
The Bonus Breeding Structure provides a vital advantage when special Monsters with limited availability are up for grabs!

The Monster-Handlers would like to extend a heartfelt thank-you for your support of My Singing Monsters! They couldn't do it without you!
data.3175446288: If it ain't broke, don't fix it - guess that was the attitude when it came time to design Paironormal Carnival's "Breeding Structure." It has the trademark 'seats' on which eggs are to rest, the mixing chamber, the chute... but it unmistakably doesn't belong anywhere but right here.
data.32347440: Lights! Camera! Breeding! Something about the way the lights twinkle and shimmer results in 25% shorter breeding times - we reckon that the swirling essences feel like they're being thrown a welcome party and can't wait to make their big debut as an egg!

data.3131784441: Want to make some Diamonds? Of course you do! Mining is a long process though, so it will take a while.
data.736070755: Time works a little differently in each dimension of reality that exists; in the Monster World, it can be manipulated to change the tempo of an island's song! Curiously, it doesn't change how long it takes to breed, or bake Treats, or anything like that - that power rests solely with the magical Diamonds.
data.1211004050: Want to make some Diamonds? Of course you do! This handy little mine never gets depleted, and is environmentally-friendly so the natural beauty of the Monster World can be preserved. Mining is a long process though, so it will take a while. When in doubt, the Monsters always recall that good things come to those who wait.
data.3985462198: The Maximum Mine spares no expense and boosts output to four times more Diamonds per day than the Mini-Mine! The Monster-Handlers would like to extend a heartfelt thank-you for your support of My Singing Monsters! They couldn't do it without you!
data.1027713815: How remarkable! Half tree/half transmission tower, having one of these makes it possible for a Monster's Happiness to be affected by their 'Likes' placed anywhere on the island! You should be happy too, since you can now decorate your island however you'd like!
data.3474117471: Singing takes a lot of energy, and Monsters need sleep! The Basic Castle really isn't very comfy at all; the rain gets through, it's drafty, and cramped.

data.424357954: After agreeing that more beds equals more fun, the Monsters set out to create the most capacious castle ever - and what better inspiration than Gold Island's own Crystal Castle? Following extensive study of the original architectural marvel, the multiverse's finest architects were tapped to craft the new castle in its image. After the smoke cleared, there was no question - this is the be-all and end-all of Monster living arrangements. Welcome to paradise!

data.1157127793: Thanks to mineralogical acumen supplied by Fire Monsters, Plant Island's residents knew exactly where to dig to find vast deposits of Vegidian, a crystal once found in abundance back when the island was an Elemental Land of the prehistoric Continent. Combining the raw strength of the crystal with modern lapidarian techniques has resulted in this magnificent Castle, furnished with more Beds than ever before.
data.3635570675: Thanks to mineralogical acumen supplied by Fire Monsters, Cold Island's residents knew exactly where to dig to find vast deposits of Frozium, a crystal once found in abundance back when the island was an Elemental Land of the prehistoric Continent. Combining the raw strength of the crystal with modern lapidarian techniques has resulted in this magnificent Castle, furnished with more Beds than ever before.
data.952807912: Thanks to mineralogical acumen supplied by Fire Monsters, Air Island's residents knew exactly where to dig to find vast deposits of Skylite, a crystal once found in abundance back when the island was an Elemental Land of the prehistoric Continent. Combining the raw strength of the crystal with modern lapidarian techniques has resulted in this magnificent Castle, furnished with more Beds than ever before.
data.3077450487: Thanks to mineralogical acumen supplied by Fire Monsters, Water Island's residents knew exactly where to dig to find vast deposits of Aquanine, a crystal once found in abundance back when the island was an Elemental Land of the prehistoric Continent.
