data.834585634: Thanks to mineralogical acumen supplied by Fire Monsters, Earth Island's residents knew exactly where to dig to find vast deposits of Stonyx, a crystal once found in abundance back when the island was an Elemental Land of the prehistoric Continent. Combining the raw strength of the crystal with modern lapidarian techniques has resulted in this magnificent Castle, furnished with more Beds than ever before.
data.2834767629: Because of their prolonged separation from the surface, and time spent underground, Fire Monsters became the resident mineralogical experts of the Monster World. While such crystals as Vegidian and Frozium still exist in copious quantities if you know where to look, the Fire Element's crystal, Pyrozite, is far rarer - only a few meager veins have survived to the current day. Luckily, the Monsters were able to scrounge together just enough from the area near the living core to fashion two Castles for the Fire Islands, with enough Beds to support their growing populations.
data.2891533095: Even though Nebulox is among the ancient Crystals that once erupted forth from the world beyond the Prism Gate, it luckily still behaves in mineralogically expectable ways! With the guidance of the local Fire delegation, the inhabitants of Psychic Island gleaned how to build a series of glorious Nebulox Castles, ones which perfectly complement the local color palette and provide the beds needed for the island's growing population.
data.2255840917: Even though Aurorium is among the ancient Crystals that once erupted forth from the world beyond the Prism Gate, it luckily still behaves in mineralogically expectable ways! With the guidance of the local Fire delegation, the inhabitants of Faerie Island gleaned how to build a series of glorious Aurorium Castles, ones which perfectly complement the local color palette and provide the beds needed for the island's growing population.
data.2466951484: Even though Quarritz is among the ancient Crystals that once erupted forth from the world beyond the Prism Gate, it luckily still behaves in mineralogically expectable ways! With the guidance of the local Fire delegation, the inhabitants of Bone Island gleaned how to build a series of glorious Quarritz Castles, ones which perfectly complement the local color palette and provide the beds needed for the island's growing population.
data.2520925925: Even though Confettite is among the ancient Crystals that once erupted forth from the world beyond the Prism Gate, it luckily still behaves in mineralogically expectable ways! With the guidance of the local Fire delegation, the inhabitants of Light Island gleaned how to build a series of glorious Confettite Castles, ones which perfectly complement the local color palette and provide the beds needed for the island's growing population.
data.4108542340: Even rarer than the sparsest minerals from the main dimension, Krystillium was the very first Crystal ever created, as a byproduct of Colossals tampering with Bubblerite. As such, deposits of it exist only within the pocket dimension. Serendipitously, the substance has the ability to impart knowledge of how to work with it to all who touch it. This trait is what allows residents of the pocket dimension to effortlessly construct Krystillium Castles with more beds than ever, and is shared with the Fire Crystal for which it served as template.
data.323197840: Because more Mythical variants were being discovered all the time, the Monsters of the dreamspace sought to make everybody a little more comfortable. Their first few suggestions left a little to be desired... Exemption from chores? Surprise unbirthday parties? Monogrammed towels? Despite the boundless imagination evident in these ideas, the best and simplest solution was, unsurprisingly, adding more beds. But how to make this dream a reality? The Cataliszt coyly revealed that its close ally in the creation of the dreamspace, the intergalactic traveler known as the Dream Crafter, has also dabbled in architecture! Sourcing the impossible mineral Hummatite that can only be formed outside reality, this ultimate Castle upgrade blends in seamlessly with Mythical Island's aesthetic... and, most importantly, brings the beds!
data.1396453708: Although shaped like a Castle, this mysterious Structure actually serves as the main power generator for the 'higher plane' of the Monster World! It's from here that the Monsters of Gold Island derive their musical inspiration, as well as their tireless energy so that they can keep singing forever!
data.3785989863: The Castle on Composer Island is styled after another famous Structure in the Monster World, but the similarity is more than skin deep! Just as the Crystal Castle serves as a power generator for Gold Island, the Composer Castle acts as a powerful transmitter for the creative energies flowing in from the human world - it's got some of the Seam's best upload/download speeds!
data.3242070240: Unsurprisingly, the Castle of Amber Island is bound by the same peculiar physics of the higher plane as Gold Island's Crystal Castle, and arranges itself in the same multi-faceted, crystalline structure as it grows skyward. What is different, of course, is the building material: hardened sap from the multiverse's World Tree, the Stablo Mjehurica, that preserves musical musings in coagulated Relic form. As more Relics are donated to its expansion, more Beds become available for the Fire Monsters of Amber Island to take up residence.
data.2997694919: If you have no Beds available in your Island's Castle but do not want to sell your Monsters, you can check them in to the Hotel. Hard to believe, but every once in a while, even singing Monsters need a break from all that singing. Luckily, this humble little hotel serves up the right kind of atmosphere for Monsters to rest and relax.
data.876168041: The addition made all the difference. Now Monsters can look forward to all the comforts of home while preserving the illusion that they're on vacation. It's very clever how they do it, something to do with the placement of furniture.
data.4284846284: Don't let anybody tell you differently, it's worth the upgrade to first class. The Grand Hotel is almost so appealing that Monsters will never want to come back out. We said 'almost.'
data.3797983348: Necessity is the mother - or should we say Monster - of invention, and when new sub-species are discovered, we want to make sure they are welcomed with the proper pomp and circumstance. With that in mind, we've bolstered the capacity of the Grand Hotel.
data.691827665: This is it! The pinnacle of luxury! You know how you can tell, besides the fact that there's physically more space to house Monsters? Neon signage. That stuff is the real deal.
data.1611474844: Doing some redecorating? Not sure what to do with that old decoration but don't want to sell it? Leave it in the Storage Shed.
data.3868808498: The Storage Barn has even more room for your decorations to live when they're not on display. The lock's been upgraded too, to make it even more secure.
data.767919767: All the space you'd ever need. The Monsters aren't entirely sure how they accomplished the feat of physics wizardry that is the Storage Warehouse, seeing as it looks the same size from the exterior. In any event, you'd best take advantage of it before the warehouse realizes it too!
data.817935919: As technology advances, we see amazing strides forward in all manner of optimization. Some of the most impressive progress has been achieved in storageology - now with more efficient ventilation, reinforced Bubblerite walls and Monster handprint tech to make sure your extra Decorations stay secure!
data.4221330826: It doesn't get much better than this! Above and beyond the accordionized roof to allow for even more Decorations to fit inside, security has been beefed up as well. Not satisfied with run-of-the-mill camera surveillance, we've upgraded the lock system to have an all-seeing eye that's actually alive! No one will dare disturb the contents of this Structure so long as it keeps watch over the entrance.
data.2259107077: The ancient magic of the Wishing Torches has been rediscovered. When Wishing Torches are lit, it improves the chances of breeding Special Monsters! This puts a whole new spin on 'carrying the torch' for someone! The power of Fire seems to hold sway over the other Natural Elements - Air, Plant, Earth, Water and Cold - and even though Torches are ignited on the surface, they appear to be drawing energy from deep below, almost as if from the Core itself.
data.2621099913: When the Glowbes first appeared, they brought with them the blueprints for a curious contraption of unknown purpose. In the spirit of diplomacy, the Singing Monsters diligently brought the plans to life, in a structure they later called the Fuzer. It was discovered that when two Glowbes entered the machine, a new Glowbe combining traits of the originals is created! This process takes some time, but luckily it seems to respond to Diamonds' magical time-bending properties.
data.2149862192: Within the blink of an eye, these mysterious beings flashed into existence and began populating the Monster World. While Singing Monsters may be in the dark about their origin (they make no sound, which completely baffles them), they have been enlightened as to their purpose: to help create amazing light displays for the islands! Glowbes have officially been welcomed into the Monster family, and are here to stay!
data.112212382: Within the blink of an eye, these mysterious beings flashed into existence and began populating the Monster World. While Singing Monsters may be in the dark about their origin (they make no sound, which completely baffles them), they have been enlightened as to their purpose: to help create amazing light displays for the islands! Glowbes have officially been welcomed into the Monster family, and are here to stay!
data.3646248439: Prolonged contact with the human world has spurred the invention of the magical Recording Studio! Now you can record and save your own version of a Monster's musical part! And if you're worried about hurting a Monster's feelings by replacing its music, don't be! Collaboration is the very essence of music, after all! Happy recording!
data.3017859918: It's a small rock. Removed rocks will be taken far away to wonderful new homes.
data.896831968: It's a medium rock. Removed rocks will be taken far away to wonderful new homes.
data.4264051269: It's a big rock. Removed rocks will be taken far away to wonderful new homes.
data.3827913760: It's a "rock" (or is it?) - neither small nor medium nor big, it's just right. Removed rocks will be reabsorbed into the essence of Zarroë.
data.1151373929: It's a small curio: some kind of a trick box with a special compartment inside. Removed curios will be cast out into the void of the Beyond... but like the others, they'll probably find their way back into orbit around the Carnival.
data.2921746187: It's a medium curio: some kind of a circus cannon used for daredevilry. Removed curios will be cast out into the void of the Beyond... but like the others, they'll probably find their way back into orbit around the Carnival.
data.1098185194: It's a big curio: some kind of a wheeled cage used to display exotic oddities. Removed curios will be cast out into the void of the Beyond... but like the others, they'll probably find their way back into orbit around the Carnival.
data.4234372279: It's a small tree. Don't worry; removed trees will be taken far away and gently replanted.
data.2063013401: It's a medium tree. Don't worry; removed trees will be taken far away and gently replanted.
data.2980824508: It's a big tree. Don't worry; removed trees will be taken far away and gently replanted.
data.1582284838: It's a "tree" (or is it?) - neither small nor medium nor big, it's just right. Don't worry; removed trees will be reabsorbed into the essence of Zarroë.
data.2943568223: The Conservatory was the sole Structure miraculously still standing after the Colossingum's momentous emergence from its hiding place. Here, Monsters can train their musical abilities to achieve new levels of songful skill. For the time being, it can only house one student at a time, but expansion efforts are being considered to improve access to more Monsters at once.
data.869362165: Now that's more like it! Thanks to modern engineering, this ancient Structure has been retrofitted to accommodate two students at once! Based on imagery discovered in its friezes, the innovation of a domed roof helps it to achieve a little more height and prominence... but could the envelope be pushed a bit further?
data.4170037840: Presto! The Prestigious Conservatory can now comfortably meet the needs of three music students at once. A study room situated on the top floor ensures that advanced students get extra special care and attention and helps the Structure to tower over the landscape and present a Tweedle's-eye view of alumni on the surface of the Colossingum.
data.2356317362: Upon resettlement of the Colossingum, the Monsters undertook the construction of a Mess Hall, the Structure where Monsters not on active musical duty can enjoy some rest and relaxation - and believe us when we say that Monsters really put the 'mess' in Mess Hall. It also serves as the communication center for the Colossingum, broadcasting motivational tunes to keep Monsters awaiting their next assignment energized and in good spirits!
data.856682858: Now that the Mess Hall has more Beds than before, more Monsters can take advantage of all that it has to offer. And taken advantage, they certainly have! Despite some of the neater Monsters' efforts to keep things tidy, it's rapidly becoming a mess in there. Oh well, Monster is as Monster does.
data.4166197967: More Beds means even more Monsters, but it has also means that the state of cleanliness in there is best left undescribed. Due to the diversity of Monster species that can co-exist in there, it's a bombardment of sounds and smells unlike much else. In situations like these, it's best to remember the old adage that 'Happiness is living in a neat mess.'
data.487676364: First created alongside Monster World cornerstones the Breeding Structure and Nursery, the Crucible uses the ancient objects of power known as Keys to literally 'unlock' the evolutionary potential within Singing Monsters. The inside of the miraculous Structure works like a sauna, getting hotter and hotter as more Relics are melted down. Once there is sufficient heat, the Elements interact with the alchemical quality of Keys and the Crucible triggers the transformation from a Common to a Rare. The process is not infallible - sometimes, multiple attempts are required, but the unfurling of Elemental Flags will always help.
data.1946817066: The capabilities of the Crucible have been maximized in this Enhanced upgrade. A bucket of sizzling Relics is poised to raise the Heat to a whole new level, gilded embellishments abound, and a red carpet welcomes hopeful Monsters aiming to achieve the highest form of their Evolution - the Epic! Be warned: the Enhanced Crucible must raise its Heat level to extremes in order to facilitate Epic evolution - and if it doesn't work the first time, all of the residual Heat dissipates instantly and an Elemental Flag furls! Additionally, more Keys than ever before will be needed to stabilize these evolutions... the power of the condensed ancient stardust from the Big Clangor contained within each Key will be pushed to its limit!
data.163357693: Many secrets of the Monster World have been transcribed and tucked away on parchment: Kayna's Map, which revealed the existence of the prehistoric Continent; or Blabbit's Scroll, which led to the discovery of Wublin Island. In those cases, it was the cooperation and resolve of special residents of the human world that solved the puzzles, so it seems appropriate that the secret to rousing the Colossals is hiding among the scraps of the Colossal Conundra, and inside a remarkable obelisk called a Coloss-Eye. Even though these Structures are newly-arrived, Bubblerite dating of the stone used in Coloss-Eyes' construction positions their age alongside other items of Monster antiquity like Monstroglyphics or Vessels. As more puzzle pieces are collected, the obelisk will be restored bit by bit to its full potential. It's said that once a Conundrum is completed and IF one knows how to 'read between the lines,' the Coloss-Eye will shudder to life and outstretch, prompting the slumbering spirit to awaken and join in the island's song. Only then, can the burden of its ancient sacrifice be lifted and the Colossal be made 'whole' once more.
data.3286841152:
The Conundrum has been completed and the Coloss-Eye has been restored! The pronunciation of this Colossal's actual name is too difficult for mankind to articulate or comprehend... but to help the feeble human mind along and for the purposes of this bio, it shall be bestowed the name Grennitch*.

Below lies an excerpt (translated from ancient Aramonic) from the Mjehuricon:
In the earliest days of the Monster World, Grennitch sought out the cosmic being that had taken up residence among the stars arranged in the shape of a crisp leaf. It had been ordained that this Colossal, like so many of its kindred, would serve as the musical instructor for these entities, and impart upon them the majesty and magic of music. When Grennitch first made contact, they were very inspired by the powerful life energy emananting from their pupil - the very cosmic dust from which it was constituted could be likened to spores on the wind. Eventually, they settled together on a 'voice' that evoked the efflorescent sounds of spring; the whisper of the wind through the reeds; the potential contained within each tiny seed; and a name. After reinventing itself as the Celestial of Plant, the Blasoom gave Grennitch gifts in return: a life-long affinity for all green things, a special communion with nature, and a deep connection to one of the foundational elements of the universe.

*This name was the winner of a "Dropping Names" Challenge during our 11th Anniversary celebration, voted on by members of the My Singing Monsters community and canonized for all time thereafter!
data.1524655866:
The Conundrum has been completed and the Coloss-Eye has been restored! The pronunciation of this Colossal's actual name is too difficult for mankind to articulate or comprehend... but to help the feeble human mind along and for the purposes of this bio, it shall be bestowed the name Frigil*.

Below lies an excerpt (translated from ancient Aramonic) from the Mjehuricon:
The Colossal Frigil had always considered themselves a loner, especially in comparison to some of the more gregarious members of the Colossal choir. It was never easy for them to socialize or find common ground or shared interests with the others, to the point where being alone seemed like the most favorable option. As such, there was a great deal of trepidation on Frigil's part when it was determined that they would be responsible for the musical tutelage of one of the future Celestials - after all, the cosmic entity in question enjoyed a reputation for being overwhelmingly friendly and outgoing. How would they get on? As it turned out, this 'odd couple' pairing proved mutually beneficial to both parties - the newly-christened Glaishur gained an understanding that shyness or introversion is not the same as being unfriendly; and Frigil learned not to try and change themselves to accommodate others. To know their worth, celebrate their weirdness and present their authentic selves. And most importantly, to embrace the wintry stillness of solitude.

*This name was the winner of a "Dropping Names" Challenge during our 11th Anniversary celebration, voted on by members of the My Singing Monsters community and canonized for all time thereafter!
data.770135660:
The Conundrum has been completed and the Coloss-Eye has been restored! The pronunciation of this Colossal's actual name is too difficult for mankind to articulate or comprehend... but to help the feeble human mind along and for the purposes of this bio, it shall be bestowed the name Zeffree*.

Below lies an excerpt (translated from ancient Aramonic) from the Mjehuricon:
Of the many Colossals who contributed to the creation of the Monster World, it was Zeffree who was most strongly enamored of gadgets and gizmos. They believed that by augmenting a being's body with cybernetic or synthetic enhancements, the resulting amalgam of organic and mechanical would be that much more impressive. One can imagine Zeffree's enthusiasm, then, when they were chosen to assist one of the cosmic beings from the universe's inception in learning music and achieving a corporeal form. During their introduction to one another, the Colossal offered countless suggestions for robotic chassis, sophisticated instruments, and the like. Yet the Celestial graciously refused them all. When pressed for a reason why, it explained that harnessing the power of the imagination was enough to manifest its creativity, and the two collaborated on a one-of-a-kind musical technique for the aptly-named Attmoz. Zeffree never did outgrow their love for gadgetry, but they now had a greater appreciation for attitudes that differed from their own. Any way the wind blows, doesn't really matter.

*This name was the winner of a "Dropping Names" Challenge during our 11th Anniversary celebration, voted on by members of the My Singing Monsters community and canonized for all time thereafter!
data.3011757007:
The Conundrum has been completed and the Coloss-Eye has been restored! The pronunciation of this Colossal's actual name is too difficult for mankind to articulate or comprehend... but to help the feeble human mind along and for the purposes of this bio, it shall be bestowed the name Hyddryd*.

Below lies an excerpt (translated from ancient Aramonic) from the Mjehuricon:
Ever since its first moments of consciousness, Hyddryd had been drawn by the prospect of adventure. The most intrepid of the Colossals, they longed to explore the furthest reaches of the cosmos, and visit every world that twinkled with the potential of life and civilization. At first, the assignment to teach one of the Celestials to sing came as a disappointment to Hyddryd, as they believed this would disrupt their adventuring. What a pleasant surprise, then, to learn that the Celestial entity with which they had been partnered was a kindred spirit, equally keen to throw caution to the wind and set sail for new horizons. So that's exactly what the like-minded pair did for some time in the early ages of the Monster World. Sometimes, this swashbuckling attitude resulted in trouble, as both were equally fearless and neither tempered the other's penchant for risking life and limb until it was too late (in the case of one limb in particular). Nevertheless, their bond was strengthened in adversity, and when Hyddryd and the Celestial of Water, soon-to-be dubbed Hornacle, finally settled on its music, it was a bright and clear clarion call that beckoned all who wander back to the familiar comforts of home.

*This name was the winner of a "Dropping Names" Challenge during our 11th Anniversary celebration, voted on by members of the My Singing Monsters community and canonized for all time thereafter!
data.3297047385:
The Conundrum has been completed and the Coloss-Eye has been restored! The pronunciation of this Colossal's actual name is too difficult for mankind to articulate or comprehend... but to help the feeble human mind along and for the purposes of this bio, it shall be bestowed the name Sollum*.

Below lies an excerpt (translated from ancient Aramonic) from the Mjehuricon:
Sollum had never had any difficulty socializing and entertaining its fellow Colossals, and was an enthusaistic contributor in the chorus. But when the ordainment came down that they would be responsible for the musical instruction of a Celestial, Sollum was surprised to feel a little social anxiety at the prospect. Performing their own music was one thing, but they weren't feeling as confident in their ability to inspire another - even though they had heard that the cosmic being in question was easygoing and level-headed. Not that any of the other Colossals would ever have known - Sollum played it off and assured everyone that it would be taken care of. Over time though, the others became aware of the Colossal's procrastination and the various excuses it devised to get out of its responsibility. Eventually, a set of circumstances were contrived wherein Sollum and the being would 'run into each other' - and with the pressures of their task out of mind, the two jammed and grooved until the Celestial of Earth, the Torrt, had settled into a comfortable rhythm. You might say that the Colossals' social experiment was a 'resounding' success!

*This name was the winner of a "Dropping Names" Challenge during our 11th Anniversary celebration, voted on by members of the My Singing Monsters community and canonized for all time thereafter!
data.121221635: The Attunement Structure is crafted from the biological material of the Workshop itself, and uses special resonance with Shards to communicate with the alternate reality of the Mirror Islands, which are brimming with Ethereal Elemental energies. Cooperative critters called Meebs are recruited to 'attune themselves' to the energy of a given Mirror Island, and to pass off that attunement to aid in the creation of new Ethereal hybrids via Synthesis. Take care: the Structure's resonance with each of the Mirror Islands varies from day to day, so some energies are more costly to collect at certain times than others.
data.717516305: The Attunement Structure has undergone an upgrade that allows it to not only continue its duty of attuning Meebs with Elemental energies, but to actively 'Tune Up' the very Monsters of the Workshop themselves into Rarethereal forms! This process has more in common with the radical, fast-paced transformations of Amber or Wublin Islands than it does with the slower-paced, natural development of Rares as a result of environmental pressures or other factors. The enigmatic biological entity housed within the Structure's 'pouch' works to target specific gene expressions of a Monster's Elemental make-up and 'Rarefy' them; as a result, Rarethereals produced in this manner tend to have a stronger spiritual presence than others.
data.1433269744: Combining Ethereal Elements in groupings greater than two is understood to be incredibly unstable. The unique conditions of the Workshop make it the only place in the Monster World where such risky maneuvers are attempted, and that's mostly to do with the presence of the Synthesizer. The 'mad science' of the Workshop reaches its zenith with this living Structure, which is responsible for stabilizing the Elemental energies from attuned Meebs into the coalesced eggs of new Ethereal hybrids. Taking care not to harm the Meebs is delicate work, and because each Element imparts a different amount of instability, certain combinations are still too unstable for the Synthesizer to withstand. Future upgrades must be considered to expand the Workshop family further.
data.1316548581: The Refined Synthesizer has been upgraded from the base form to accommodate for the instability present in more advanced Triple combinations, and can even manage the least unstable Quad-Element configuration (albeit with some difficulty). The payoff is this advanced Synthesis process requires an already-coalesced form to build upon, so a Triple-Element Ethereal must be prepared to undergo the transformation of a lifetime to accomplish it. That, and more Attuned Meebs and Shards are needed than ever before. But no matter - in the name of science, progress cannot be halted and innovation never curtailed! Onward!
data.2233744448: As the Synthesizer continues to undergo refinements that allow for increasingly unstable Ethereal Elemental combinations to be formed, the living Structure itself appears to be changing as well. The 'hose' that connects the tumbling Meebs has lengthened to accommodate greater energy demands, and the glow from the central chamber seems to have shifted in hue as well. Most notably, the modest bulb near the top that began as a container for some mysterious chemical agent has swelled to a more impressive size, and now houses... something else.
data.2552302840: Refinements continue to reshape the Synthesizer as we know it. Perhaps owing to the increased strain of targeting more and more unstable Element combinations, the Synthesizer now has a protective 'door' across the main aperture to protect from radiation. The bulb up top has further expanded in size to accommodate the mysterious organism within, and a warmer, more vital color is beginning to seep outwards from it into the criss-crossing stems on the Structure's exterior. The color of these living stems matches the 'trees' that dot the landscape of the Workshop, definitively highlighting the Synthesis process's reliance on all things organic to achieve greater stability.
data.1400758109: Nearing its final stage of refinement, the Mega-Super-Hyper-Refined Synthesizer is positively bursting with vitality! The verdant hues emanating from the upper bulb have spread throughout the Structure, and the organism lurking within can barely contain itself. The woody stems encircling the central chamber now offer a more solid frame, perfect for synthesizing among the most unstable Elemental combinations known to Monsterkind!
data.3588861427: Refinement: complete! With these finishing touches to the Ultra-Mega-Super-Hyper-Refined Synthesizer, the most unstable Elemental combinations known to the Workshop are within reach. But the budding blooms atop its winding branches aren't the only life form growing strong... the mysterious organism lurking within the upper bulb has continued to harvest nourishment from the expanding orchestra of the Workshop. Will we ever come to know its true nature?
data.1508260468: So-named for the series of steps it creates to summon its sectaries, the Stair Shaper is a life form alien to the Monster World. That said, it shares some important similarities with the Colossals: its aspirations to create life and its musical affinity. Thus, it came to devise the novel mode of transportation called Transposition: it can vibrate matter at a different musical key and cause it to 'step' up or down to access new physical states. The statue that resides on the Nexus is but one of many forms that the Stair Shaper can take, but this form is best-suited to receive Transposed Magicals and vibrate them at the proper frequency to ensure maximum compatibility. Don't panic - even though the sensation of being Transposed to a different state of being can be disorienting, it's worth the trip.
data.2161823540: The Nucleus resides at the very center of the Magical Nexus - the intersection of multiple worlds and whims. Another of the Stair Shaper's creations, this Structure serves as a conduit of the Magical energies that accumulate as more Monsters are Transposed to the Nexus. It's able to accomplish this by absorbing their energy signatures and converting them into a tangible form. When enough of a particular group of Monsters are Transposed, a section will light up to signal their unification. If its shape looks familiar, that's because the diverse Star Rocks of the Monster World are actually the Stair Shaper's handiwork as well; left behind as monuments to 'first contact' and obtainable only through Starpower, the essence that amasses when harmonies from differing worlds are sung.
data.2826054138: Just as a chord in music theory can be 'augmented,' so too can the Magical Nexus's Nucleus Structure. This is accomplished with a technique akin to (but not exactly like) Transposition - one major difference is that the augmentation requires Shards, which we thought could only be produced in the pocket dimension... and rather than 'lifting' the whole item, only part of it gets lifted... ?... it gets kind of complicated to explain (this is true of music theory in general), so perhaps it's better to just smile and nod. The important thing you need to know is that the Structure's ability to display unified Monster populations with glowing Rhombi is still intact - now with extra sparkles. Who doesn't like sparkles?
data.1060968100: Another of the Colossal Zarroë's ingenious inventions, the Dish-Harmonizer is made up of the same biological material that gave rise to the Attunement Structure back in the Workshop. First, the Quad-Element Ethereal of the Islet's host Element must be teleported to the target island (a Whaill on Plasma Islet, for example) and be reborn from an attached Egg Cup (a kind of Nursery replacement). The Structure then executes the miraculous process known as Dish-Harmonization, returning the synthesized Quad back into a state of 'Primordial Soup'. The will of Zarroë manifests with a mighty squish until only the originating Meebs are left, and few traces of the input Monster remain. Here's where the genius comes in: the reborn Meebs retain their Elemental Attunements and freely float and bounce in the gloopy matter of the Dish. Finally, the Meebs recombine with other Meebs to form other Ethereals, reuniting Monsters of various stability levels for the first time! There's even a special mechanism that allows the missing Ethereal Element to be conjured from the other four! While it certainly looks appetizing, we ask that you please do not sample the Soup.
data.3114886154: Onwards and upwards! Like the name suggests, the Rarefied Dish-Harmonizer has been upgraded to have the chance to produce Rarethereals as possible products of the Dish-Harmonization process. The Primordial Soup contained therein is unchanged, but the organic material drawn from Zarroë's own essence has been fortified to entice Rare 'potential' to be incorporated into the outcome Monsters. However, this can only be harnessed during times when Ethereal energy levels spike in the Mirror TImeline.
data.1097000359: The dual Egg Cups attached to the Dish-Harmonizer have all of the nurturing properties of a Nursery's straw bed and heat lamp, for the fraction of the size and energy usage. Amazing what you can do with technology nowadays (if you're a mad scientist Colossal, that is).
data.4188168375: Delivered surreptitiously as an attachment on the Wubnet, the Polarity Amplifier is the pièce de resistance of Wublin Island's Electric tech. As the number of unique Monsters awoken or powered up on the island increases, so too does the Stage (or power level) of the Polarity Amplifier. At each turn, this novel Structure extends the range of a Wublin's positive Polarity, and reduces the range of its negative Polarity, all while the sophistication of its floating rig increases as well. The stupendous light show for which Wublin Island is known is also synced up to both this Structure and the island's boisterous song.
data.42550686: Whoever studies Monsters should see to it that in the process they do not become a Monster. And if you gaze long enough into an abyss, the abyss will gaze back into you...
data.1335066945: Whoever studies Monsters should see to it that in the process they do not become a Monster. And if you gaze long enough into an abyss, the abyss will gaze back into you... but the Centri-Fugue proves that it isn't always a bad thing! The origin of this mysterious, mirror-like Structure is unknown - we may never know if it came into being at the same time as the Paironormal Carnival, or if its existence actually predates the island's formation. Equally enigmatic are the purplish spectral hands that seem to be manipulating its movements. What we do know is that this is the mechanism that Paironormals can use to fill out with their opposite form on the other side of the Carnival, to form a 'Pair.' This is true of both Major Paironormals lacking their Minor form, and vice versa. As a Paironormal stares deeply into the reflection, it tumbles headlong into the world of the Centri-Fugue and gets spun around using 'Centri-fugual force.' The act of Fuguing is not always successful, but there is a consolation prize for having entered this 'Fugue state' - the Monster gets fed with some Treats! Who knew that this event was catered?!
data.2102771110: Legend has it that an ancient Monster created this Reflecting Pool to see if it had any spots on its back. Time has worn away the stone details and vines have grown around it. We still don't know about the spots.
data.2975205707: Who can resist the charm of an old bridge over a tinkling freshet, all in the shade of a drooping Snuffle Tree?
data.1568666668: This Digger looks like it's emerging from the earth. How exciting!
data.1901426038: It's a hollow log. Maybe something cool will move in.
data.4119610299: Someone has captured the elusive Wild Bagpipe! It's been partially buried to prevent escape.
data.2708061258: Who doesn't love to let their flag fly? Even better if it flaps and snaps in the wind!
data.2287538889: Curious Monsters often sing to the Fossil-osaurus, hoping to wake it up. Little do they know that it's just a fiberglass model from some long-ago time.
data.248340583: Monsters are known to plant flowers in and around Eerie Remains to show respect for the deceased.
data.916208674: Monsters generally agree that this is a ridiculous fantasy creature. Nonetheless, they still make wishes and toss their teeth in the fountain.
data.1327785229: It's nice to have a place to take a quick nap. The Cozee Cabin isn't nearly as comfy as the castle, but it'll do in a pinch!
data.3383760803: Some Monsters like to haul their stuff around with them and need a place to put it for the day.
data.3434819877: Many Monsters find the dark pretty scary. They'll never need to turn this light on, but it's comforting just to have around.
data.1244611467: Two very different objects seem to have materialized in the same place!
data.1403383807: Monsters love to throw small objects into the Bottomless Pit. Sometimes an interesting sound or smell comes back up!
data.1319165767: Not for the faint of heart, a Toob oozes fragrant black goo. Monsters delight in daring one another to give the wheel a spin!
data.3417660318: The Pipes of Cicado have amazing summoning power. When they are blown, everyone comes from all around, no matter what they may have been doing.
data.1093393720: The Harpsitree plays hauntingly beautiful melodies to anyone nearby. You'll often see a Monster quivering its lower lip or gushing fat tears as it listens to a particularly moving passage.
data.3351228310: When an unwary Monster trips over the strings of a Guitree, the most delightful sights and sounds are produced!
data.1588824433: When a strong breeze blows, the Castanevine really comes to life. Monsters sometimes blow at the plant just to hear it play.
data.863006131: What could be more comforting than cuddling with a ripe, orange Smunkin? Just don't let any Thumpies near; they like to carve out faces on them. What a strange thing to do.
data.2385889219: A real Spurrit has not been spotted for a very long time. In fact, no one has ever seen one... or have they?
data.3845672184: Monsters love to interpret the mysterious meaning of this symbol. None of them can agree on a single way to read it, however, and the purpose is entirely unknown.
data.1672232534: Beware! This sign likes to play switcheroo with its arrows. Where you're going all depends on where you are.
data.2101095202: While it may look delicious, eating Fire Bush leaves has dire effects, causing the consumer to produce eye-watering flatulence.
data.1638918252: This statue immortalizes the bravery with which its model saw something scary, pointed, and knelt in fear.
data.744966503: The Dragoon was a harmless creature with a poor understanding of effective camouflage. Its resemblance to a bodiless claw made it such a prized collector's item that it rapidly became extinct.
data.2491431536: What unfortunate creature gave up its hide for this device? None! This very-much-alive domesticated critter suspends itself from a frame and hangs there. No one knows why.
data.474208950: Yum Yum Trees jiggle delightfully when gently shaken. Caution is advised, however, as the globes break easily, drenching the shaker with a sap as sticky as bubblegum.
data.3609135379: Monsters should know better than to look directly at a Beeyoot Tree. When observed, the tree goes full-on supermodel, posing and contorting. Weird.
data.3390716331: The Fuzzle Tree loves it when Monsters have long picnic lunches under its branches. Sometimes it grabs a snack from the basket when no one is looking.
data.21424654: This tree has the highest accident rating of all the trees. Its feather-like foliage tickles anyone who attempts to climb it until they fall out laughing.
data.2278725792: Zuffle Trees bear delicious fruit that closely resembles the material pulled from a hairbrush. Those who consume it produce a melodic gacking sound.
data.1284393733: Monsters decorate Piney Trees every year for the Festival of Yay. Much to the tree's relief, Monsters do not decorate well, and the objects fall off immediately.
data.1988373109: Monsters must be careful around the Saggle Trees. Their tasty nuts are too delicious-looking to resist, but they taste like rocks. Yuck!
data.4160516105: Don't look too closely at the Bloofi Tree. Its feathery leaves have been 'borrowed' for some unknown purpose, leaving embarrassing bald patches.
data.1017160620: Old timers say never to go near a Crumpler Tree at night or you will face the wrath of the Crumplings, invisible fairies that leave you covered with itchy bites. What do old timers know, anyhow?
data.3124023554: Razzli Trees attract lots of things; iridescent bugs, slithery slugs, and especially Monsters. Up close, the tree is kind of boring, and all the creatures leave.
data.4012847416: This tower would be more fun if it would just stand still and allow itself to be climbed! Still, it looks pretty.
data.1773880214: The umbrella roof makes this a great tower for rainy days.
data.2733084723: Made from old dragon parts the Monsters had laying around, this tower isn't very practical, but it looks great!
data.2400124984: Hailing from the Shard Mountains of another dimension of the multiverse, this interesting specimen has been encased in amber. It answers to 'Flutter,' but the Monsters usually just call it a Thing.
data.569882503: Mischievous creatures, Floofies are sometimes mistaken for sock fluff. Have you ever seen sock fluff jump around like that?
data.3015328587: The grimacing figure in this sculpture is based on a figure from Monster bedtime stories, who would bathe and clean misbehaving Monsterlings until there was no more dirt and grime on them. What a terrible fate.
data.4285890432: For those who can't decide between regular Bubblerite and its mossy variant, this is the best of both worlds!
data.2044792110: This Bubblerite tile has been tinted green with moss recovered from an Entbrat's horns.
data.2998787723: Carved from polished Bubblerite, this groundpath is smooth and soft to the touch. But watch that those bubbles don't trip you up!
data.2948132403: Nothing like the feeling of soft flowers underfoot. What about the Monsters without feet, you might ask? We may never know the feeling.
data.1692679574: A collection of wild Moshrooms have pushed their way up through this stone path.
data.3799011128: These leaves have fallen off Big Trees. We'll get around to tidying them up someday. In the meantime, they make a wonderful path!
data.690789533: Better watch your step! Baby Inverdigus plants, who begin their lives as eyeballs on the forest floor, are watching you too.
data.3631885896: These old stones have been here since forever. Not sure where they came from, but they don't seem to be going anywhere either.
data.321322477: It's right there in the name - there are triple planks, hence Triplank. Any similarity to the hide of an Epic Entbrat is purely coincidental, we can reassure you.
data.2455676817: Did you know that a cross-section slice of a tree is called a cookie? This type of cookie may seem less appealing to you, but trust us that Monsters can make just about anything work as a snack.
data.3161017237: The stubborn Inverdigus plant insists on being different... it grows with its roots in the air and foliage on the ground! Despite what you may think, it's still good at sticking in place.
data.1119818574: Who thought it was a good path to make a path out of ice? Don't slip up!
data.3291177440: This must have been thought up by the same genius who created the Icy Path.
data.259502661: Part of the Cold Colossal's bushy hair has poked through the island surface. Good thing it's so soft... no complaints here!
data.309494525: What's this? Some vestige of when the Cold Colossal dyed its hair purple. It was just a phase.
data.3643691352: Somehow, this patch of ice always displays the reflection of a beautiful aurora, even when none appears in the sky.
data.1606034422: Someone had the fun idea to dye Bubblerite ore blue with a Deedge's earwax. So if blue bubbles tickle your fancy, this is the groundpath for you.
data.2498162771: These footprints in the snow only kind of resemble the feet of some Cold Island denizens... so maybe it's a prank? We wouldn't put it past them.
data.1706050182: Should we be worried that these cracks have appeared in the surface of the island? Things appear stable right now... but don't go sticking any acorns in there, just to be safe.
data.3221777998: The rare Cryosanthemum flower can only be found in certain isolated pockets of Cold Island. Needing only ice to flourish, its stalks resemble upturned icicles.
data.2670316235: Some clumsy Mammott tripped while carrying some miracle hair-growth formula (don't ask, it's a sensitive subject) and this is what happened.
data.431863909: These bubble-like orbs sporadically emerge from the Wild Bagpipes behind the Air Colossal's ears.
data.3537988544: Shards of floating rock gather together in little groups like this. Why? To share secrets, of course.
data.3487849336: Don't look now, but it appears a Dandidoo's florets have blown off its head and landed in a pile on the ground. Tough break.
data.79202525: The leaves of the trees on Air Island are very ticklish... they can really make you yuck it up!
data.2183985779: You'd think it would be uncomfortable to tread on thistles, but miraculously, it feels wonderful!
data.1232087510: These collapsed Wild Bagpipe reeds double as little luxury condominiums for Air Island's beetle critter grubs. With that in mind, take care not to crush them underfoot.
data.3089537795: The gigantic goggles of the Air Island Colossal are sometimes in need of repair, so these spare parts kicking around come in handy. Just keep an eye out for any scavenging Cybops.
data.1560776504: The peculiar physics of Air Island prevent these bubbles from floating away... besides, they are quite content to stick around to serve as this charming fence.
data.3797601427: These fronds sway back and forth in the ocean's current... staring at them for too long can make you pretty sleepy.
data.1691270717: Monsters sometimes use this reed-like coral as straws for their fizzy drinks. Resourceful!
data.2945609112: A family of seastars has selflessly created a little pathway for you. Aren't seastars just the nicest creatures you ever met?
data.2996264224: Some experts believe that this type of coral developed spots to camouflage in with its surroundings. Not so. Turns out it just has Monster pox.
data.2043383429: How is there a puddle underwater? Oh well, don't question it too much.
data.4284480555: These green Bubblerite pebbles would be really good for skipping. Unfortunately, skipping pebbles tends to work better on the surface than underwater.
data.872624014: There's nothing quite so beautiful as the pearlescence of an underwater seashell. Well, actually - there are many beautiful things, but this one ranks way up there!
data.3310722395: Thanks to the jellyfish critters' malleable bodies, they can comfortably rest in a squashed position upon the ground and safely be trod upon.
data.952772088: This kindly sea creature has volunteered to stand guard - well, more like 'sprawl' guard - on this watery post. How polite.
data.1070339350: Strangely, these flat stones are always found in groups of nine. Safety in numbers? They get stepped on, all the same.
data.3109578680: Nothing too fancy. Gets the job done. Don't pop these bubbles, though - the stain will never come out.
data.1912910877: When the heat of Earth Island gets to be too much for some Monsters, a cool Firechill tile can provide instant relief. Does it look hot to you? That's just the paintjob.
data.1862370469: This plank of wood is hot to the touch. That's because it's made from the Torchle Trees that grow on Earth Island.
data.2757580544: Kind of like a ninetile path... except... there's only one.
data.583607726: Looks are deceiving. These tiles are actually the heating elements on the stovetop of some underground kiln. Go figure.
data.3918919179: Are your feet cold? Stand over a Sizzler for a while... guaranteed, you will never be cold again!
data.415450334: If you love the appearance of rocks underlit with an earthy glow, this groundpath has your name on it. If not... well, we don't know how to tell you this, but...
data.3550451579: Grateminds think alike, or so they say. But beware of groupthink, because paths aren't known for their critical thinking skills.
data.2781201550: Distantly related to Quarristers, this living rock has much humbler aspirations. Barrockades feel totally fulfilled in performing the function of a fence.
data.1603313757: Bubblerite is found throughout many dimensions of the multiverse, including the pocket dimension of Ethereal Island. This teal variety perfectly complements its indigo landscape.
data.3640972019: Ethereal Monsters harvest the moldy gunk between their toes to paint bubblerite this lovely shade.
data.307757398: When Bubblerite path tiles break down from Monsters treading on them, no one bothers to clean up the pieces because they look so pretty. This path design skips the treading step.
data.257765870: Along with the Glyphic path, this mysterious symbol is all that's left of a prehistoric Monster civilization. It's believed to signify the duality of Ethereal Monsters being able to exist in the Natural universe and the Ethereal plane.
data.3288457803: Monster historians have speculated about this marking's true meaning for ages. Most conclude it represents interdimensional teleportation, but some radicals purport it's instruction on how to make the best ice cream cone.
data.1117098213: Rarely, a slab of Bubblerite will contain only one of the bubble-like formations that give it its name. It is a singular pleasure for a Monster to pop the bubble, which results in a fractured tile.
data.2311668544: Unlike the gray matter familiar to you and I, the brain tissue of a Colossal often takes on a purplish hue when exposed to Ethereal energies.
data.2023747989: This porthole is covered by a window-like film that allows Monsters to gaze down into the depths of Ethereal Island... I wonder what's down there?
data.2027976040: Perfectly round, Waferocks are only formed in interdimensional geological conditions when two universes converge, like in Shugabush Island.
data.4269017030: The curious Lignaline tree produces lumber that arranges itself along geometric lines and patterns without any help.
data.891868259: When Shugabush Island was formed, these mystifying artifacts tumbled through the dimensional wormhole and scattered everywhere. The Monsters can't decide what they're used for, but the leading theory is that they're the wheels of a spacetime-faring craft.
data.674096347: It's suspected that the true purpose of these outerdimensional discs is known only to the members of the Shugafam, who share a connection with their universe of origin.
data.3815847806: The musical Decibur plant relies on interdimensionally-traveling Monsters to spread its seeds, contained inside burs which attach themselves to Monster fur and which give a loud squeak when squeezed.
data.1709524432: Inspired by the Leafstrewn Path of Plant Island, these leaves are locked in a perpetual state of wonderful autumnal crunchiness.
data.2931398261: Shugabush says that these unusual Dya-frram formations in the Rocks and Trees of Shugabush Island can be detached and set in the ground to help the song rock even louder!
data.1609508000: The wires embedded in the ground are believed to be connected to the giant earbuds sticking out of the Shugabush Island Colossal's ears. But that's just a theory...
data.3841742032: During the many years of separation from their brethren, Fire Monsters took solace in the glow of the Unsnuffable Outcropping, which features candles that never get extinguished. To them, this symbolized that they would one day be reunited.
data.36055997: Deep underground, the familiar Bubblerite of the surface mixes with wax-like deposits to form Waxerite. Monsters then mold the new mineral into tiles that are very comfortable to tread upon.
data.2226280723: Sometimes Waxerite gets too close to an open flame and gets a little singed. Not to fear though, because the new 'burngundy' color still achieves a lovely aesthetic.
data.1341033142: Using pieces of rock that have tumbled down from the ceiling of their cavernous refuge, Monsters jump around pretending that the floor is lava! No surprises there, since actual lava is close at hand.
data.1391180302: Monsters love to write secret messages to each other, which they later conceal with a layer of golden Waxerite. Sometimes, they pry them up to read the messages, but other times it looks so nice that they remain unperturbed.
data.2578934187: Having been cut off from the rest of the Monster World for so long, the rock sometimes emits some pretty funky fumes. But what might offend us smells perfectly lovely to a Monster nose.
data.522394373: As a token of their excitement for being reunited with Fire hybrids, the Monsters from the world above brought with them a supply of Bubblerite pebbles. Strangely, when exposed to the air of Fire Haven, they all turned red!
data.3565144224: The waxy surface of Fire Haven sometimes gives way to vent hot air rising from the smoldering depths of the living core. If you're standing over it while it happens... let's hope that you're a Fire Monster and enjoy that kind of thing.
data.623475317: This pool of magma bears a striking resemblance to the dorsal reservoir of a Rare Sneyser, and is most certainly just as broiling to the touch.
data.2167537168: Miraculously, the island surface of Fire Oasis functions perfectly as a marver for even more glassblowing! Multiple colorants were added to achieve this particular work of art.
data.2136288741: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed it into a path tile! This Turquoiseglass is almost as vibrant as the Wynq's fur-like plumage.
data.4190199627: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed some into a path tile! This Orangeglass reminds us of the Sneyser's leathery hide.
data.849186030: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed some into a path tile! This Greenglass even outshines the gleaming scales of the Phangler.
data.798538838: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed some into a path tile! This Purpleglass is a deeper shade than any of the colors found on the Whaddle.
data.3838078963: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed some into a path tile! This Redglass is so vivid, it's almost driven the Woolabee's birds to distraction!
data.1649417565: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed some into a path tile! This Yellowglass is bright, but it doesn't hold a candle to the brilliant flight feathers of the Glowl.
data.2836188920: The Monsters had some blown glass left over after the formation of Fire Oasis, so they repurposed some into a path tile! This Blueglass is richer in color than the paler coat of the Boskus.
data.1482325037: This is a miniaturized version of one of the blown glass platforms that supports Fire Oasis's Structures. Standing on it can make you feel special and important, too.
data.2466688904: 'Frit' describes the small pieces of colored glass that are used to add visual interest to blown glass creations. The more you know!
data.2730751072: This sloughed scale has been carved into the shape of a square, but it got a little damaged in the process. It's a rarer color than most of the other scales found around here - maybe it has some special significance?
data.609715918: This sloughed scale has been carved into the shape of a square, but it got pretty damaged in the process. It's a rarer color than most of the other scales found around here - maybe it has some special significance?
data.4010497387: This sloughed scale has been carved into the shape of a square, and is in excellent condition. The majority of scales that are found around here share its plum color.
data.4061029843: This sloughed scale has been carved into the shape of a square, but it got pretty damaged in the process. The majority of scales that are found around here share its plum color.
data.961721974: This sloughed scale has been carved into the shape of a square, and is in excellent condition. It's a rarer color than most of the other scales found around here - maybe it has some special significance?
data.3217474776: This sloughed scale has been carved into the shape of a square, but it got a little damaged in the process. The majority of scales that are found around here share its plum color.
data.473833318: One of the many tentacles encircling this island has broken through the surface and reaches skyward. Upon approach, one can feel a faint sensation, but the feeling passes just as quickly.
data.2594860488: One of the many tentacles encircling this island has broken through the surface but remains partially buried. Hands off, if you know what's good for you!
data.1375125101: One of the many tentacles encircling this island has broken through the surface but remains mostly buried. Fragments of scales are still fused to its gelatinous exterior.
data.1956255613: A Cruv'laaphtian tentacle has curled up in a shape reminiscent of the Psychic Element sigil. Stare at it for too long at your own risk.
data.2244781480: Thanks to the specific psionic setup of these pebbles, you can hear them talking in your mind! Well, I can, at least.
data.520722606: This Skipstone is Magically-enhanced to skip across the surface of a Faerie Island pond for much farther than regular physics would permit.
data.2577263104: These Skiprocks are Magically-enhanced to skip across the surface of a Faerie Island pond for much farther than regular physics would permit.
data.1388394917: These Skippebbles are Magically-enhanced to skip across the surface of a Faerie Island pond for much farther than regular physics would permit.
data.1338247453: Faerie Elementals share an arcane knowledge of semi-precious stones with Magical properties. Roobyooty inspires Monsters to see only the inner beauty of others, even if their exterior is gruesome or unsettling.
data.2224609976: Faerie Elementals share an arcane knowledge of semi-precious stones with Magical properties. Deposits of Tacitrine can instill passerby with feelings of peace and quiet, but silence for Singing Monsters can be a little eerie.
data.34383894: Faerie Elementals share an arcane knowledge of semi-precious stones with Magical properties. The presence of a Pasapphire can almost instantly settle any disagreement between Monsters, and even make them forget what they were arguing about.
data.3377494963: Faerie Elementals share an arcane knowledge of semi-precious stones with Magical properties. You'll know if you're anywhere near a Ticklepink pocket, because of the uncontrollable giggling and guffawing you'll be doing.
data.939968870: Faerie Elementals share an arcane knowledge of semi-precious stones with Magical properties. Ephemeralds can influence one's perception of the passing of time - entire days can pass by, and it will have only seemed like a brief moment.
data.4082768579: A patch of the same vegetation that makes up Faesodd's mask has sprouted. The eye pattern that appears on its leaves is as pulchritudinous as it is uncanny.
data.1914847423: Just like the ring of mushrooms that encircles Faerie Island as a whole, this fungal circle has magical properties.
data.3265216811: The fossil record of Bone Island indicates that there was once a subterranean pool nearby that supported life, like the remains of the congenial Ammonice.
data.1141584773: It's still unclear whether or not this was a multi-legged bug or a flattened frond. What is clear is that it's neat to stand on.
data.2404900896: Make no bones about it, these bones have been arranged in the shape of the Magical Element's sigil.
data.2454900888: Believed to be only part of the vertebral column of a prehistoric beast, it remains our only clue about the shape and size of the creature.
data.1494090557: These little bones have been configured to form part of a little trail! Where does it lead, I wonder?
data.3751440787: These little bones have been configured to form part of a little trail! Where does it lead, I wonder?
data.348561974: These little bones have been configured to form part of a little trail! Where does it lead, I wonder?
data.3851423971: These little bones have been configured to form part of a little trail! Where does it lead, I wonder?
data.785171270: It's Bone Island's favorite clastic rock station, bringing you the hits of yesterday and today. Necrull tunes in all the time.
data.2947848506: If there's fossil evidence of the Ammonice, it was only a matter of time before the Ammonaughty was discovered. The Monster World must remain in balance.
data.3857683486: There's an old legend that states that the Sparkul Path is made up of special particles that descend from the Aurora Monstralis above - or was that legend about the Glittuh path? Always confusing those two.
data.1669064368: An old legend relates that the Glittuh Path is a photochemical reaction with the shining lights of the Aurora Monstralis above - or was that legend about the Sparkul path? Always confusing those two.
data.2821141781: The Phosphora Titan's head is covered with glowing growths that resemble Rocks and Trees. These nodules begin to develop beneath the surface of the Titan's skin, in special dilatable openings called Ocherpores.
data.3038905773: Not to be confused with Cyanopores, Siennapores contain nodules in the prime of their development and poised to emerge from the surface of Phosphora's head. At this stage, they emit heat as well as light.
data.2122241544: Not to be confused with Siennapores, Cyanopores contain nodules that never rise and instead enjoy a long, slow and peaceful decline until they are reabsorbed.
data.4176127142: Rather than push through the surface of the Phosphora Titan's head, some nodules stay underneath and grow sideways instead. These tend to be tender, and even make a small groaning sound when stepped on.
data.867596035: The Angroan Path is an Ingroan nodule that has gotten, shall we say, angry. Even when there's no Monster standing on it, it can sometimes be heard grumbling and whining.
data.3269510614: Very rarely, a glowing nodule that can't break through the surface forms a strange pattern called an Eksboks on the ground. It's sort of a console-ation prize.
data.163321459: Instead of one big opening like Siennapores or Cyanopores, clusters of holes called Glowrifices will sometimes dot the surface of Light Island.
data.2294565903: Would you look at that: a sprinkling of dust descended from the Aurora Monstralis has collected in a little dollop - delightful!
data.947473819: This path displays the Ethereal sigil, and represents the first inhabitants of the pocket dimension of the Monster World... Never forget those who paved the way! It was presented to the Monsters of Magical Sanctum by their neighbors on Ethereal Island.
data.3203219253: This path displays the Magical sigil, and represents the newest inhabitants of the pocket dimension of the Monster World... There's always room for new talent! This symbol has been co-opted by the Monsters of Magical Sanctum, but its first recorded appearances can be traced all the way back to the Thumpieverse.
data.1974594704: This path is believed to display the Prism Gate, the first-recorded interdimensional portal sighted in the Monster World all the way back during the Dawn of Fire. Long since dormant, nowadays it only opens for new Glowbes Conjured from their home dimension.
data.1756683304: Teleportation is one of the oldest phenomena in the Monster World - at one point, it was the only way to get around! The slab used for this path bears remarkable geologic similarities to the stones quarried for the ancient Teleporter... although it appears to be inert and incapable of initiating Teleportation on its own.
data.2749894541: More stones, seemingly from the same quarry as the Teleportal Sign Path, have been cropping up. Interdimensional hitchhikers through time and space? Maybe!
data.628915491: The antlers of Enchantler, the Titan-Who-Would-Be-Colossal, aren't the only thing coursing with Monstromantic energies. Even its dense fur occasionally glows, but the area is a little tender afterwards. Let's just hope there aren't any itinerant beings Wander-ing around who might want to climb up the sides of the Titan's body.
data.3995119238: The antlers of Enchantler, the Titan-Who-Would-Be-Colossal, aren't the only thing coursing with Monstromantic energies. Even its dense fur occasionally glows, but the area is a little tender afterwards. data.3559575542: This is maybe a little gross, but this is actually a big clogged hair follicle of Enchantler. At this scale, though, it's not too obvious.
data.1431475594: If the texture of Magical Sanctum's fuzzy terrain isn't to your liking, why not spread a Tasselrug for your pocket dimension picnic?
