data.1413427956: The same enigamtic creature that takes refuge inside the Epic Viveine's chest cavity also makes its home on Seasonal Shanty, using unknown methods to gouge the wooden floor and peer out from under the planks.
data.2748438499: Carved from a special variety of Skylite and inlaid on a section of Green Tree wood, this path tile displays the contemporary style of the Air Element's sigil. Over time, the sharp angles of the traditional sigil gave way to smoother shapes and more numerous lines.
data.625321293: Carved from a special variety of Vegidian and inlaid on a section of Green Tree wood, this path tile displays the contemporary style of the Plant Element's sigil. It's largely unchanged from its traditional design, in stark contrast to the ever-shifting life cycles of plants in the Monster World.
data.3994646248: Carved from a special variety of Stonyx and inlaid on a section of Green Tree wood, this path tile displays the contemporary style of the Earth Element's sigil. Unlike the traditional design, its pieces are broken apart - perhaps a commentary on time and its erosive effects.
data.4078716496: Carved from a special variety of Aquanine and inlaid on a section of Green Tree wood, this path tile displays the contemporary style of the Water Element's sigil. As if to represent water's permanence and importance, it's nearly identical to its traditional design.
data.943756789: Carved from a special variety of Frozium and inlaid on a section of Green Tree wood, this path tile displays the contemporary style of the Cold Element's sigil. It far more closely resembles an actual snowflake than the traditional design's more abstract approach.
data.3201623899: Carved from a special variety of Pyrozite and inlaid on a section of Green Tree wood, this path tile displays the contemporary style of the Fire Element's sigil. A more full-bodied representation of the Element, but not all in one piece - not unlike the diasporic range of its Elementals in the modern Monster World.
data.1971860734: The Air Element sigil's design on this path tile is meant to emulate that of another 'relic' of the ancient Monster World - the lofty mechanism of Starhenge. The original was crafted from crystallized Celestial energies, and based heavily on the constellation of Weathered Winds and its cloud form.
data.2229175851: The Plant Element sigil's design on this path tile is meant to emulate that of another 'relic' of the ancient Monster World - the lofty mechanism of Starhenge. The original was crafted from crystallized Celestial energies, and based on the constellation of Growing Green and its robust leaf.
data.1333941646: The Earth Element sigil's design on this path tile is meant to emulate that of another 'relic' of the ancient Monster World - the lofty mechanism of Starhenge. The original was crafted from crystallized Celestial energies, and based on the constellation of Earthen Ellipse and its mountainous silhouette.
data.3472494578: The Water Element sigil's design on this path tile is meant to emulate that of another 'relic' of the ancient Monster World - the lofty mechanism of Starhenge. The original was crafted from crystallized Celestial energies, and based on the constellation of Ocean's Tears and its pair of droplets.
data.94821463: The Cold Element sigil's design on this path tile is meant to emulate that of another 'relic' of the ancient Monster World - the lofty mechanism of Starhenge. The original was crafted from crystallized Celestial energies, and based on the constellation of Frozen Form and its irregular number of plates.
data.2201136889: The Fire Element sigil's design on this path tile is meant to emulate that of another 'relic' of the ancient Monster World - the lofty mechanism of Starhenge. The original was crafted from crystallized Celestial energies, and based on the constellation of Core Aflame and its hollow base that represents the living core.
data.1215200604: In a move that some Monsters considered a little hubristic, Kaynas took the symbol that adorns the Amber Colossal's central bead and created a variant form of it to represent themselves.
data.1433095652: Yes, we know that the berries themselves aren't green, but these leaves are derived from the Green Trees of Amber Island. So that's why they're called Greenberries - even though they're reddish-orange.
data.2118585958: Often appearing alongside other ancient forms like the Elgnairt, Erauqs and Ssorc, the Elcric shape is said to have special significance in dreamcraft. This path is fashioned from the same specular Hummatite as the structure platforms of Mythical Island.
data.4174609608: Often appearing alongside other ancient forms like the Elcric, Erauqs and Ssorc, the Elgnairt shape is said to have special significance in dreamcraft. This path is fashioned from the raw Hummatite ore that gets carved to make elements of the unique spires of Mythical Island.
data.865054573: Often appearing alongside other ancient forms like the Elcric, Elgnairt and Ssorc, the Erauqs shape is said to have special significance in dreamcraft. This path is fashioned from the same diffuse Hummatite as the circumferential trim of Mythical Island.
data.780836821: Often appearing alongside other ancient forms like the Elcric, Elgnairt and Erauqs, the Ssorc shape is said to have special significance in dreamcraft. This path is fashioned from a rare silvery Hummatite variety used to absorb moon energy to light up the Lunapparatus.
data.3856026736: Norewop is a mineral ore polymorph of Hummatite with special glowing properties. Despite their individual potencies, Norewop is needed to activate the dreamcraft potential of the four ancient shapes.
data.1665285854: The two moons of the Mythical sigil represent the curious nature of its Monsters of 'belonging' to more than one world. Here, a single moon is depicted to allegorize their final, singular existence on Mythical Island.
data.2820599163: Repmub is a mineral ore polymorph of Hummatite with special reflective properties. The dreamcraft potential of the four ancient shapes can only truly be achieved when a pair of Repmubs have been partnered with them.
data.1497915310: A Rekaeps is the nickname for a small aperture in the surface of Mythical Island's artificial construct. When grouped together, Rekaeps seem to be able to amplify the volume of the island song, if nearly imperceptibly.
data.53741630: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. Meeboid material has a similar color and consistency to Meebs themselves, but is actually inanimate - it's the same substance used to craft the giant Meeb statues and obstacles.
data.2241886864: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. Stroma is the spongy protoplasmic framework that acts a supportive tissue that encircles the Workshop.
data.1325181237: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. This photoreceptor cell is but one of many that make up the Colossal's multiple compound eyes.
data.1408866701: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. Endothelial cells regulate the transfer of chemicals around the Workshop, and form the blood-brain barrier (or BBB for short).
data.2560969256: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. Glia are the unsung heroes of the nervous system, providing support and protection for the synthetic cube-shaped 'neurons' found in various vaults.
data.506542214: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. Monstrochondria are the powerhouse of the cell... but you probably knew that already. They reside in stasis, floating in cytoplasm and prepared to defend the Workshop from being discovered by the outside world.
data.3580750627: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. Besides their organic utility, the reticulated patterns of cell structures make for stunning-looking groundpaths. A marriage of form and function!
data.607869430: The Colossal Zarroë has deigned to allow the denizens of the Workshop to use some of its 'building blocks' as paths. These vessels traverse the interior of Ethereal Workshop, and help to transport bubbles (among other things) from one location to another.
data.1796354913:
Remember how we said that trees and rocks that are removed from your islands will find new homes elsewhere? Good news - these are somebody else's trees and rocks and your islands are their new homes! Recycling is awesome.

This Decoration is locked in its regular appearance, and is not affected by Seasonal Events.
data.1730729423: Harvesting the bounty growing from these trees may seem innocent, but remember: you're basically plucking the Harvherd Titan's hairs. Ugh, toma-totally grody!
data.2689207436: So if the Harvherd Titan is composed of fruits and veggies, what does that make these rocks? Skin tater-tags? Ingrown pears? Oh my gourd!
data.3549504525: Swetis have evolved conical tree-like growths to offer brave travelers extra footholds on their journey. Hanging colorful flags adds extra visibility in whiteout conditions. Just don't look down!
data.1015240446: The Cliffmunk critters call these rocks home. The domed temples make a cozy getaway from the blizzards that befall the Settlement whenever a Sweti scratches its itchy scalp.
data.3311238435: Need a place to stay the night? Look no further than the Cheep and Cheerful family of hotels, motels and inns! The bigger the tree, the better the star rating.
data.1946123991: These charming little nests were once home to great Gigacheep fledglings! Now that they're all grown up, the nests make a perfect starter home for other furthered friends looking to start a family of their own.
data.195802971: The waters surrounding the mysterious Glubber Lagoon have their own chemical composition, courtesy of the Glubber's, um... secretions. These conditions nurture unique vegetation found nowhere else in the living ocean!
data.3799151866: Glubbers covet treasures above all else, yet the saline depths of the living ocean do not make the most hospitable storage space. While these treasures may have corroded, they have since assumed a new role as hiding spots for shy sea creatures.
data.2112844264: These elaborate stone structures are complex traps designed to only admit adventurers clever enough to solve their puzzles. Just what was Hahoo guarding behind these devices? No one has been quick-witted enough to find out.
data.2486612553: Excavating the Temple's sands has uncovered a number of intriguing artifacts and inscriptions of ancient origin. Just how ancient, you ask? Well, if we could read them, maybe we'd know!
data.1939833099: Dark matter from the Rift is responsible for the sticky pink residue draped on this tree, from when the Ethereal energies of the pocket dimension leaked into the main dimension of the Mirror timeline.
data.2018703069: It's nice to think that even after the upheaval of Outer Islands crashing into the Mirror timeline, this tree not only survived, but was able to incorporate some new aspects into its Magical Island aesthetic.
data.3036502088: In the Mirror timeline, the Ethereal energies of the pocket dimension have encroached upon the main dimension. When they did so, dark matter from the Rift left behind the sticky pink residue found on rocks like this one.
data.3207606174: Visual elements from both the Magical Island Titan and the conjoining Outer Island are visible in this rock; a product of two worlds previously thought impossible.
data.1524814381: It's a small curio: some kind of a trick box with a special compartment inside. This one was plucked from orbit and has come down to resettle on the Carnival grounds.
data.2959347535: It's a medium curio: some kind of a circus cannon used for daredevilry. This one was plucked from orbit and has come down to resettle on the Carnival grounds.
data.1597421998: It's a big curio: some kind of a wheeled cage used to display exotic oddities. This one was plucked from orbit and has come down to resettle on the Carnival grounds.
data.4117344723:
Someone has strewn toilet paper all over the trees to celebrate the spooky season... we'd bet our bottom Diamond it was the Oaktopus.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Spooktacle Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2607926703:
Deck the trees of Cold Island with tinsel and ornaments to make the joyous spirit of the holidays last.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Festival of Yay Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.3772364788:
Every year around this time, Air Island's Yucka Tree thistles take on the rosy hue of love and whisper romantic poetry to each other by rustling their branches.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Season of Love Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1887382128:
Looks like the Blabbit's handiwork. I wonder if the Monsters that will one day hatch from these eggs will be colorful as well?

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Eggs-Travaganza Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.529512073:
The normally bent and burnt Torchle Trees of Earth Island have been festooned with summery attire. They really put the hot in 'hot fun in the summetime.'

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the SummerSong Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1858271184:
The rustic charm of this Feast-Ember Tree brings to mind a freshly-baked Pie on a cool afternoon. Sure, Fire Haven never gets cool, but a Monster can dream!

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Feast-Ember Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2906206859:
Because the soil of Bone Island has a hard time supporting flowers other than the cempasúchil, beautiful metallic blossoms have been affixed to the unusual 'trees' of the underground habitat.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Beat Hereafter Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1124137208:
Mushaboom's green thumbs (or whatever you want to call them) have been busy helping this little plant to thrive! The 'lucky bamboo' grove isn't actually bamboo at all, but a species called Mon-drake-aena, which is said to bring luck in the coming year.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Crescendo Moon Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.4036503809:
So that's where Ffidyll sources the wood for its instrument from! These 'Treefoils' can only grow around the time of the vernal equinox, and afterwards the fallen timber is hewn to fashion the Seasonal's fiddle.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Cloverspell Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1386686554:
The favorable conditions of the higher plane allow many species of tree from the Greatfruit Grove to thrive. Even at different stages of their life cycles, Plantelope, Halleberry and Scorchberry trees all offer up sumptuous, ambrosial fruits of indescribable flavor.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Echoes of Eco Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2321441221:
These already stunning pieces of blown glass are now the perfect merger of art and science! In need of field equipment on the go? It's as simple as plucking what you need from the tree, as you would any flower.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Perplexplore Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.679817400:
Prototypes of the trees in the pocket dimension were 'mocked up' using plasmic Krystillium suspended in place by Electricity, not unlike fulgurites. While they're a little unsettling, they're perfectly at home on the eerily otherworldly Ethereal Island.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Life-Formula Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1864426132:
The usual xanthous glow of Phosphora's 'trees' has been dimmed so as not to compete with the impossibly beautiful craftwork that now adorns their branches. They're the perfect microcosmic reflection of the spectacularly-sized fireworks erupting in the background.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the SkyPainting Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.3528234886:
Even though these objects continue to defy categorization as proper 'trees,' they are at least getting in the spirit of MindBoggle by curling up with a good book to read or a brain-teaser to solve. Despite the fact that they are seemingly eyeless, we're told the glasses still help.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the MindBoggle Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.839608464:
Rocks aren't the most talented at dress-up - the most they've managed is to become a little darker. However, the local arachnids have offered a hand (they have many of them) to add some cobwebs to complete the look.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Spooktacle Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.977897234:
There's this popular opinion going around that rocks make undesirable presents... this rock is here to prove otherwise. Look at that lovely bow, who could possibly refuse?

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Festival of Yay Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1450947584:
Even the rocks are trying to break in to the love scene... I'm told no pebble can resist a good 'boulder smoulder.'

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Season of Love Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2577852881:
Many theories exist to explain the origin of these strange statues. Some suggest that they were deposited by some ancient race of Monsters. Others contend that they depict the famous Shellbeat musician Gene Krabba. I guess we'll never know for sure.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Eggs-Travaganza Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.4132861224:
Not to be left out, the rocks of Earth Island got all summered up as well. Now they look absolutely delectable! Be warned, though, it's only skin deep - they still taste like rocks.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the SummerSong Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.4015569580:
You may be tempted to jump in this crunchy leaf pile, but you better think twice. It's still a Rock, after all!

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Feast-Ember Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.748912631:
Crystal sugar from deposits in the stalactites and stalagmites of Bone Island are sourced to create the sugar skulls which adorn these rock formations.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Beat Hereafter Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.3795366469:
When you're setting a resolution for the new year, you can use all the luck you can get! That's why branches of this special red fortune fruit have been arranged in gilded stone vases (and the best part: they're artificial so the luck doesn't run out!)

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Crescendo Moon Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.423750304:
Is it me... or did that rock just move? The magic that hangs heavy in the air during Cloverspell enchants the long-dormant trolls, gnomes and sprites of Faerie Island and allows them to shift their positions every once in a while - what a relief it must be to be able to get a good stretch in!

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Cloverspell Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2525618533:
As if by some herbological miracle, plant life erupts from the Amber chunks strewn about the island. Perhaps it can be traced back to the life-force of the multiversal trees, or else the Rocks' special similarity to the physiology of the Fire Elemental.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Echoes of Eco Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.728116088:
Maintaining a strong sense of direction and knowing the time of day are integral aspects of any successful exploration. Whether it's maps, sundials or compasses, these items are never in short supply with these handy objects around.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Perplexplore Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2650064835:
These sculptures represent early drafts of Monsters designed by the Colossals... but unlike Monculus, they never progressed to the animation stage. They stand as a testament to the ingenuity of their creators, and double as containers for valuable crystal ores.

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the Life-Formula Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.3662747119:
These multi-faceted formations remind us of the colors and configuration of the Prisments that encircled the ancient Prism Gate. Coincidence?

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the SkyPainting Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.1406589690:
The many eyes of Cruv'laapht have joined the summer reading sensation, but it's a shame that they're mostly farsighted, because it means they have to rely on magnifying glasses or monocles. Nothing can deter them, however - where there's a will, there's a way!

This Obstacle reverts back to its regular appearance when the MindBoggle Seasonal Event concludes, except if the Island Skin has been purchased.
data.2119830533:
Someone has strewn toilet paper all over the trees to celebrate the spooky season... we'd bet our bottom Diamond it was the Oaktopus.

Miraculously, this Decoration is able to retain its Seasonal appearance all year long, even after Spooktacle has ended.

Deck the trees of Cold Island with tinsel and ornaments to make the joyous spirit of the holidays last.

Miraculously, this Decoration is able to retain its Seasonal appearance all year long, even after the Festival of Yay has ended.

The many eyes of Cruv'laapht have joined the summer reading sensation, but it's a shame that they're mostly farsighted, because it means they have to rely on magnifying glasses or monocles. Nothing can deter them, however - where there's a will, there's a way!

data.850545977: This Spooktacular item is perfect for brewing potent potions and mysterious mixtures. We thought that those were bundles of wheat behind it - but maybe they're the shed silks of the Rare Oaktopus? It is a mad scientist, after all.
data.3022405527: A chilling collection of eerie artifacts from the Spooktacle season. That skull bears a striking resemblance to the Mythical Jarhead, a fearsome-looking entity that Monsters still aren't sure whether or not they dreamed up.
data.2138762290: The spooky yet breathtaking Inverdigus plant is truly a sight to behold. Mostly because you can bet it is using its baleful eyestalks to behold you at the same time. This full-grown specimen has been carefully perched atop some decorative headstones and adorned with a fake shrunken head. At least, we hope it's fake.
data.1652546698: Around the Spooktacle season, Monsters love to hollow out Smunkins and carve expressive faces into them, transforming them into Smunk-o-Lanterns. It is imperative that Monsters double check they aren't trying to pull up a sleeping Punkleton from the ground.
data.2532539059:
Inspired by the exclusive PlayMonster Decoration available with Skeleton Mammott in our online store

Beware! This sign has gotten into the spirit of the season - you never know quite whether you're going to get a trick or a treat.
data.900139510:
Inspired by the exclusive PlayMonster Decoration available with Furcorn the 13th in our online store

Looks like no one heeded our advice and let Thumpies free in the Smunkin Patch. Just as we feared, they carved out faces on them. Strange.
data.3500542993: This trio of Coldmen (not snowmen, mind you) are content to converse amongst themselves. The cold never bothered them anyways.
data.1446149823: The area around this lamppost was reportedly the ancient stomping grounds of a curious entity called Sir Sunmut. It was always babbling on about spare rooms and wardrobes, for some reason.
data.2641276186: Modeled after the scrumptious seasonal coverings that adorn the Cold Colossal's icy horns, this edible edifice boasts stockings filled to the brim with surprises.
data.2154536354: There's so much seasonal imagery in one Decoration! Bows, poinsettias, holly, ornaments - placing one of these on your island is a sure-fire way to keep the Yay spirit going strong.
data.960004157: Ninety-nine dreams we have had; in every one, either a purple, pink, or red balloon. These inflatable baubles emanate the power of Luff.
data.3215782547: This unusual object collects motes of dust floating through the sky, and transmutes them through arcane alchemical processes into candy hearts! Is it alive? We're not sure.
data.1961894198: The dilapidated fence surrounding this exotic plant isn't for its protection, it's for yours. More serpentine than rosaceous, it's not just thorns to be wary of on this specimen.
data.1777668494: Yum! Hewn from the Love Rocks of Air Island, this contraption has an elaborate mechanism for pumping liquid chokkolit to the top, which then flows down the sides for sweet-toothed Monsters to sample.
data.165972676: Because they already sort of look like eggs, the Floofies thought they'd conspire with the Blabbit to play a clever joke and get dressed up like Singing Monsters! How crafty of them.
data.2406545514: In the Monster World, being called a rotten egg is considered a compliment! After all, it means that you've been around a while and no one has thrown you out yet!
data.1143745487: Pareidolia is the phenomenon that describes seeing faces in inanimate objects, like this clearly-not-alive carrot of ginormous size. See, it even has a bite out of it. There's no way it cou-...
data.1495866231: Some painted eggs are more special than others. This 'Phabberjay,' as it's called, features depictions of beings from across several dimensions of the multiverse.
data.2206673014: Ahh, nothing like stretching out on a relaxing beach chair. Remember to apply that sun screen - and try not to damage those surfboards, they feature vintage designs.
data.85154520: While Singing Monsters prefer castles large enough to accommodate their beds, Granular Castles play host to all manner of miniature sand-dwelling critters. Looks like they're having a party in there!
data.3461319037: A Monster's nose knows not which way to wend when sniffing the Hut of a Thousand Perfumes. Exotic fruits, fragrant flowers, ambrosial oils - all in all, an odoriferous experience for olfaction.
data.3544889797: Relax in the bubbling waters of this all-natural Hot Tub. A surprising number of Monsters can fit in it at once, so we suspect there's some magic at work here.
data.2475074753: This balloon is from Fire Haven's welcome back party for Gobbleygourd, during the Seasonal Monster's first return to the island since its rediscovery. It has been specially inflated for Feast-Ember, but take care that there are no leaks anywhere... that would be embarrassing.
data.353556079: Despite their name, Scareglowls actually attract Glowls, not drive them away! The Fire Monsters will spot them from on high and swoop down to try and make conversation, only to discover that the prospective conversationalists are stuffed with hay.
data.3729655242: What is it with Seasonal Decorations and pareidolia, seeing faces in inanimate objects? This particular object is maybe a little more animate than one would hope, so stick your hand in for a snack at your own risk.
data.3276486002: Since it keeps frequent company with Fire Monsters, Gobbleygourd knows all sorts of exciting recipes from the era of the Dawn of Fire. Apple Tart, Pumpkin (ancestor of the contemporary Smunkin) Pie... although maybe someone went a little overboard on the portion sizes.
data.3834414274: This candlelit altar holds drawings and keepsakes of lost loved ones. Full-scale ofrendas typically consist of 3 tiers, but the Monsters are hoping that the aroma of the cempasúchil adorning this makeshift display will be enough to draw the spirits back to the land of the living.
data.1645803116: A beautiful guitar too large for any Monster (except one that's been Biggified) rests gently on its stand. As the subterranean breeze blows, it passes through the papel picado and over the guitar's strings, stirring up nostalgic feelings of kinship and warmth.
data.2839815625: Lady Calaca, Monsters at your feet... wonder how you manage to make ends meet! This towering statue is made of fired clay and enrobed in festive attire to celebrate the Beat Hereafter. Bone-Element Monsters have special techniques known only to them to achieve craftsMonship at this scale.
data.3024164209: A small figure soundlessly mock-plays a special drum... but where's the beat? This is a reference to the heartbeat of forever, which can only be heard if your heart is open and you are listening very carefully.
data.16057398: This column of floating lanterns is decorated with imagery of fantastical creatures from other realms and clouds in the traditional xiangyun style. It's believed the lotus base emanates not only honesty, purity and beauty, but also the faint levitational power required to keep the lanterns afloat.
data.2254534296: Just like the Leafy Sea Dragon, the 'Lion' is widely regarded as a fantasy creature, existing only in legend. The difference is that while the former is merely a speculative work of art, this jade sculpture is imbued with heavenly spirituality and resonates with a powerful blessing for all those nearby.
data.1295885629: It's a tradition during Crescendo Moon to deliver red envelopes containing good luck wishes and "money warding off old age" (instrumental in Carillong's perpetual youth). The grandiose cutout display symbolizes a koi's journey upstream to become an auspicious dragon, the paragon of power and strength.
data.1345885573: Peonies are considered to be the king of flowers around the lunar new year, radiating wealth and good fortune. This circular 'wheel' arrangement, surrounded by vases and lucky bamboo, evokes a round window through which one might catch a glimpse of the upcoming year's success and prosperity.
data.2960794867: What size of creature could this shoe possibly accommodate? A juvenile Titan, perhaps? Touching the cool, metallic surface of the shoe confers a blessing of good luck - but only for a limited time.
data.921589341: At the rainbow's end, you'll occasionally find a pot like this one. Make no mistake - these are not the same Coins that are in circulation elsewhere in the Monster World. These are enchanted pieces that tantalize passersby but which dematerialize when they are removed from the pot.
data.4256335352: Rather than being some kind of mechanical marvel, this 'contraption' is actually powered by magic alone; a potent spell has been cast upon it. A Cloverspell, if you will.
data.3770135872: Using dyes collected from verdant sources across the Monster World, these cupcakes of unusual size are the perfect color and flavor to celebrate the vernal equinox! Some have theorized that the Magical Rooba's diet consists entirely of these - but it turns out that's just moss.
data.1476793984: All that fertilizer for the plant life of Echoes of Eco has to come from somewhere, and the Bovine Pegacorn is more than happy to oblige. Curiously, the Outhouse resembles Amber Island's Crucible - there's even a shape carved in the door of each - although their purposes vary drastically, as do the smells emanating from them.
data.3734135854: The eponymous tree of Greatfruit Grove looks a little different depending on what dimension it's grown in, but an effort has been made to capture the spirit of the original with a carving bearing a jubilant face. The flavor of the fruit that this tree bears rivals that of any in the Monster World.
data.365868939: This is a miniature version of the Germination Station from Greatfruit Grove, where trees of all types were cared for and cultivated. Based on the purple and white leaves, there's currently a Polycule sapling in the planter - or it could be a Bazoom tree; honestly, it's hard to tell at this stage.
data.147556147: In its native dimension, Greatfruit Grove was tended to by a troop of 'adorable little genius monkeys' who lived in the main tree, which they called the Monkey Mansion. These monkeys were responsible for the splicing that gave rise to so many fruit varieties, and the Golden Simian depicted on this fountain decoration was one such monkey.
data.3683000898: Finally, a working model of the toroidal Monster World has been created! The moon's orbit passing through the center of the torus is achieved with the help of the various compages that encircle the planet, and the last recorded position of each of the islands of the main dimension has been illustrated in detail. A scientific achievement of epic proportions.
data.1561481452: Heavy is the head that wears the helmet, and it's nice to feel the breeze on the top of one's head. The Spurrits prefer to be consistent in who wears which headgear, but that's not to say that they don't sometimes switch off - sometimes a change is as good as a rest.
data.2521727817: After a long day's work, it's nice to relax by a campfire and observe the various constellations of the Monster World. Marshmallows are a staple of the Spurrit diet, because they don't present any challenge to the Monster's toothless mouth.
data.2336994289: This is the mobile command center of the Spurrits' ongoing studies of the Monster World. All of the Seasonal Monsters' gadgets and instruments are contained therein, and because they have successfully integrated into Monster society, there's no need to camouflage it. As such, the tent is a striking blue color (Spurrit's favorite).
data.1777069358: Monsterkind has long pontificated about the creator - or creators? - behind these mysterious Graven Ghazts. A perplexing prophecy passed down through generations suggests that once the saucer of each statue is fitted with its matching orb, a passageway to another dimension will reveal itself.
data.4018118528: Invented by the genius Rare Reebro, the Versegazing Telescope sees not just across distances, but dimensions! While its potential for making Island-shattering discoveries is second to none, its most common use is leisurely Tweedle-watching.
data.606293029: The Teleportal connects the fabled pocket dimension to the outside world. Its arrival is heralded by the appearance of a strange runic symbol seen throughout Monster antiquity. While countless semioticians have tirelessly researched its meaning, its true significance may be lost to time.
data.958823581: From sketch to sculpture, the Blueprint Workshop was once the birthplace of your favorite Monster species. The Colossals used all manner of tools, Bubblerite builds and Krystillium konstructs to craft Kazilleons and forge Fwogs, resulting in artisanship that was the envy of all during the Dawn of the Fire. Some craftsMons even modeled their own Workshops after the original!
data.2122518802: Did you know that Glowbes are bright in more ways than one? Within a Glowbe's big ol' globe lies a treasure trove of fun facts. This radiant Decoration honors Glowbe-kind's perpetual pursuit of the most titillating trivia they can uncover.
data.4162282428: Be sure to stand clear of the Fireworks Stand when it's showtime! While its flashy colours and sparkling lights may draw you in, all it takes is one lit fuse for the whole kit and caboodle to burst into an unforgettable performance of booms, bangs, and beauty. It is a beloved SkyPainting tradition to sneak surprise messages into your firework designs at special celebrations.
data.860598297: The Kaleidoscope is not only a mesmerizing art installation, but also a reminder that every Monster looks at the world through its own lens. When all of these wondrously different perspectives combine, they create a magnificent mosaic that is much more than the sum of its parts. Many a Monster has lost track of time gazing into the Kaleidoscope's peaceful patterns.
data.776896673: It's not a toy, it's a replica! This mint condition collector's item represents an impeccable imitation of the famed Prism Gate from Monster antiquity. This coveted piece even features a complete collection of all 6 Prisment accessories. The Prism Gate's transformative qualities continue to spark the curiosity of Monsterkind to this very day.
data.2705004860: No Monster knows for certain what will transpire once the final puzzle in the Brain Teaser Tome is solved. Will the puzzler be showered in a rain of Diamonds, or stuffed with all the Cakes they can eat? Shockingly, the deep, dark truth is even more mindboggling: there is, in fact, no end to its puzzles. When one teaser is completed, a new page quietly appears.
data.665807762: To this day, no Monster has found the true meaning behind the complex symbols of the Confounding Cryptex - though countless have tried. It is widely believed that the solution lies within the fabled 'Dip Vinci Code', a sequence of Dipster sounds that will spring forth a special Key to unlock the Cryptex's hidden treasures... at least, in theory. The Dipsters prefer to mind their own Keys, thank you very much.
data.3975395383: Why settle for a boring ol' stuck-on-the-ground library when you can have a floating one? The Floating Library is the perfect hangout for airborne (or spaceborne) Monsters looking for a relaxing read. Just don't expect to find your books in the most logical sections; with tomes flying every which way, it's not the easiest library to organize.
data.4059465871: Ooo Ay La Bib'leo-Theque? Right here! Part bookcase, part Bib'leo hangout, the Bib'leo-Theque is where restless Bib'leos go to make friends, have a nap or just snack on some chewy old bookmarks. You wouldn't know by looking at them, but Bib'leos are actually pack-oriented creatures who find comfort in company.
data.32444944: The spooky yet breathtaking Inverdigus plant is truly a sight to behold. Mostly because you can bet it is using its baleful eyestalks to behold you at the same time.
data.2711964831: It can take a while to get used to the Jarhead. But the Monsters keep it around because despite its fearsome appearance, it has a gentle heart. Even though they're pretty sure that it only has a brain.
data.1851388481: Because the Wubbox is part machine, it sometimes needs to replace the pieces of its body that have been worn down by the Electric energy coursing through it. Other Monsters have taken it upon themselves to set up tarpaulin lean-to's to protect the Wubbox's spare parts. Mysteriously, the markings painted on the tarp appear all by themselves.
data.2131724782: Carved out of lumber harvested from the Great Tree of the Thumpieverse, this totem pays tribute to three famous Thumpies: KarrToof, RhooTz, and ChoMPh.
data.158926130: This relic isn't from around these parts. After a few experimental licks, it was discovered it tastes like grape bubblegum. The visages carved into its surface occasionally emit a low hum when monsters aren't looking.
data.3628385665: It doesn't take long for a resourceful Monster to grab what's nearby and turn it into an instrument! The Log Drum is a perfect example of this.
data.1989708254: Who needs a unicorn when you could have a twihorn? Distantly related to pitcher plants, this is more of a pitchy plant. Keep practicing.
data.983160479: Jumping on a Tamborine Trampoline is a great way to spend the afternoon, but if you're looking for a noiseless activity, this ain't it.
data.3315278237: Only the Gaung Tree has branches strong enough to support the weight of this ornate gong. Something to do with the unique metallurgical composition of the disc.
data.1184571383: Is that a Clamble you spy? No, but it's thought that this sparsely-leaved but plate-plentiful plant is a different branch of the same family tree.
data.2122694399: Even though Monsters are focused on training their musical abilities on the Colossingum, it's nice to take a breather and enjoy some refreshments. Watch your fingers, though.
data.94529589: This fountain was created to exalt the adorably fierce critter of the Colossingum, who bounces among the columns and vaulted chambers to stay in 'fighting' shape.
data.3774185166: When the Monsters first arrived on the Colossingum, they discovered a tickle trunk's worth of Costumes to don for their musical competitions. Although they are typically stored inside the Mess Hall, this on-the-go Costume Rack makes quick changes a breeze!
data.3437702597: After dismantling the Spurrit of Adventure sailboat to aid in the construction of the Mess Hall, Mammott, Toe Jammer and Noggin sought to preserve the memory of the faithful little vessel that bore them safely to the Colossingum. This little model does exactly that!
data.3323510454: One of the few surviving items other than the Conservatory, this Torch appears to lift Monsters' spirits and put them in a wishing mood whenever they're near. It possibly represents the first time that the magic of the Fire Element was used to create a Torch with powerful potential.
data.857000165: This previously-unnamed plant used to thrive during the era of the Dawn of Fire. The hedge's growth resembles a tangle of clarinets, so in our inifnite wisdom, we decided to call it a Clarinedge. Aren't we clever?
data.3865872319: The Ottavine is a distant ancestor of the Castanevine, and has a notorious reputation for being finnicky about its growing conditions. We'd call it high-strung, but highfalutin might be more appropriate.
data.2337807762: It doesn't take long for a persistent Monster to grab an unconventional object and turn it into an instrument! The Stone Drum is a perfect example of this... okay, maybe not a perfect example, but we liked how it went hand in hand with the Log Drum.
data.953047486: This unusual tripodal instrument sounds like a cross between a dulcimer and the rumbling, gurgling sounds a stomach makes when one is hungry. Hence the name Strummy-ache.
data.2072101642:
This is a plaster cast reproduction of one of the columns in the Conservatory, covered in records of the ancient Monster World transcribed in the Monstroglyphic script. This translated excerpt is the first of four:

During the waning years of the Dawn of Fire, two Colossals were troubled, worrying that peace and stability in the Monster World couldn't last forever. While both believed that the best way to prepare for the future was to strengthen the musical abilities of the Singing Monsters, they disagreed on how best to go about it.
data.4246057380:
This is a plaster cast reproduction of one of the columns in the Conservatory, covered in records of the ancient Monster World transcribed in the Monstroglyphic script. This translated excerpt is the second of four:

One Colossal, known as Lanysta, contended that individual training, conducted in a special environment and focused on honing each Monster's unique musical craft, was the answer. Lanysta found a deserted landmass and imbued it with its lifeforce to create the Colossingum, where Monsters could empower themselves to become better performers in the Conservatory.
data.910778881:
This is a plaster cast reproduction of one of the columns in the Conservatory, covered in records of the ancient Monster World transcribed in the Monstroglyphic script. This translated excerpt is the third of four (but damage to the original column makes the inscription incomplete):

This was later revealed to have been a timely decision, as it was not along after t-
(inscription missing)
-age was done, splintering the Continent into many pieces and signaling the end of the Dawn of Fire.
data.726422201:
This is a plaster cast reproduction of one of the columns in the Conservatory, covered in records of the ancient Monster World transcribed in the Monstroglyphic script. This translated excerpt is the fourth of four:

The other Colossal, known as Ahymnsa and inspired by the actions of their friend, chose another landmass to shift their power into, taking along a piece of the amber containing an important figure to act as the island's centerpiece. There, Ahymnsa created a prophecy that foretold of a time when Monsters singing together and being united in song would help to restore balance in the Monster World.
data.412960070: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Grennitch's Gambit Quest! Grennitch 'roots' for all Monsters brave enough to represent Plant Island on the Colossingum... but is especially proud of its Champions: Potbelly, Entbrat, Punkleton and Ghazt.
data.1838170196: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Frigil's Feat Quest! Frigil considers all Monsters brave enough to represent Cold Island on the Colossingum to be pretty 'cool'... but is especially proud of its Champions: Mammott, Deedge, Yool and Grumpyre.
data.3339119760: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Zeffree's Zenith Quest! Zeffree thinks 'highly' of all Monsters brave enough to represent Air Island on the Colossingum... but is especially proud of its Champions: Tweedle, Riff, Schmoochle and Reebro.
data.3125979590: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Hyddryd's Hurdle Quest! Hyddryd knows that all Monsters brave enough to represent Water Island on the Colossingum are 'making waves'... but is especially proud of its Champions: Toe Jammer, Shellbeat, Blabbit and Jeeode.
data.1095800087: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Sollum's Stratagem Quest! Sollum supports all Monsters brave enough to represent Earth Island on the Colossingum... but is especially proud of its Champions: Noggin, Quarrister, Hoola and Humbug.
data.1111614247: This magnificent crown proudly displays your accomplishments in the Champions' Guild on the Colossingum. Bravo!
data.3957410412: This magnificent crown proudly displays your accomplishments in the Champions' Guild on the Colossingum. Bravo!
data.395245424: This magnificent crown proudly displays your accomplishments in the Champions' Guild on the Colossingum. Bravo!
data.499848079: This scale model of the special stage commemorates 8 amazing episodes of My Singing Monsters: Fandemonium! It's a balanced combination of Monster ingenuity and 'hooman' technology, perfect for broadcasting from the Monster World right into the comfort of your home!
data.713788281: This Decoration is a scale model of one of the rockets and launchpads from the 'Blast Off' game, which can be found inside the virtual world of the Playground inside of a Wubbox. Get ready for liftoff... oh dear, not enough energy. Maybe some other time. Up, Up and Away!
data.2887776727: The Hub world of the Playground (or as it's coloquially referred to, the 'Wub Hub') is full of whimsical edifices like this one. The architectural styles of these buildings are inspired by designs from across the Monster World, but motifs and patterns from the archaic Starhenge seem to abound - unsurprising when you consider who designed the Wubbox...
data.1732455026: This Decoration is a scale model of one of the racetracks from the 'Monster Rally' game, which can be found inside the virtual world of the Playground inside of a Wubbox. On your marks... get set... GO! Wow, a photo finish! Pure Skill!
data.2051414730: This Decoration is a scale model of one of the cannons and cannonballs from the 'Cannon Chaos' game, which can be found inside the virtual world of the Playground inside of a Wubbox. Because it's powered by Electricity, maybe the Playground can connect digitally to other dimensions of the multiverse; we wonder if this activity was inspired by a visit to Toppelopolis?
data.3595840013:
Designed by Anthony F.

Originally constructed as a tribute to Colossals and Titans alike, this work of art now houses many small creatures. Combining the Fire and Magical Islands into a Miniature Island Complex has created a Utopia for these tiny beings to flourish. Whether it's looking through the windowed eyes, or taking a relaxing dip in the Sanctum Swimming Pool atop the structure, there is something for all these little critters to do - including some kind of invite-only shindig that Crumplings are always crashing.
data.1354782883:
Designed by GHOSTYMPA

The existence of the Optiglowbe indicates that there are more varieties of Glowbe living in their home dimension than previously reckoned! This specimen appears to be more advanced than the others - some have speculated that it is related in some way to the critters of Psychic Island, and may even share their ability to surveil and record its surroundings.
data.2610727686:
Designed by Nicolas Kelsey

The Mini-Shrine, or 'Shrini' for short, is an august creation of unknown origin which has the power to grant Monsters a vacation to the destination of their choosing. The opportunity doesn't come without some effort, though - a Monster must first collect tufts of the Sweetstreamz Tree, along with a special item that hints at where it wants to vacation. Then, it must deposit them in the bowl of the Shrini, strike the gong with the mallet, and POOF!... the Monster will dematerialize and travel to their vacation spot.
data.2258222014:
Designed by MneneT

What is this? Is it one of Wublin Island's numerous batteries? Is it an updated model for Galvana's staff? A Dyson Sphere? A Van de Graaff Generator? Or is it simply a cluttered agglomeration of different parts from a low budget sci-fi movie? Probably the latter, but it still looks cool!
data.1304782875:
Designed by Kabwax

The identity of the mysterious figure Kab has been hotly debated for eons - almost as hot as the bubbling wax held inside its famous buckets! The leading theory is that Kab is a time- and space-faring artisan that once proffered counsel to the Colossal of Amber Island. While in search of special capsules for Fire Monsters' essence, Kab's wax-sealed vessel design was chosen as the most viable. The entity's influence is felt even in the modern era, as the Fire Monsters of Fire Haven feel instinctually compelled to create art in the form of buckets and fill them with the wax of the island's many candles.
data.3411090101:
Designed by TOADBRINGER

Gaze upon the majesty of the Macho Monument! This statue (named Steve) depicts a strongmon (NOT named Steve) and showcases what diligent training can accomplish. Some allege that the monument is actually an early draft of the Wubbox that never progressed any further. Either way - allow this fitness tribute to spark inspiration in your heart, so that, one day, you can be a Macho Mon too!
data.903440:
Designed by Deep Grewal (Deepysingh800)

The Harmonorb is an odd object. Randomly found one day, the Monsters don't know what it is, but Monsters near it have felt more musically inspired than ever! So a replica has been made of it as well as a replica of the holder the Monsters made to, well, hold it. This replica has all of the engraved, pretty patterns, and bits 'n' bobs the original holder had! The original orb and holder has influenced Monster culture for generations. Just one thing - where did this strange sphere come from? (It couldn't have come from the hooman world though, that's just a silly conspiracy theory!)
data.4049304517:
Designed by GPPYE

This Teleporter's unstable portal appears to connect this world to the alternate dimension of Cave Crystal Island, where an enigmatic creature called the Connatruna dwells. The island occasionally flickers in and out of view - perhaps at the whims of the magical gem that floats above it - so take care as you're stepping through that you don't actually teleport yourself somewhere else!
data.973580384:
Designed by Quazuk and TheCybopGuy

This creature, or "Fortuitously Unidentified Monster," has been right under our noses for... who knows how long! It is rumored that Dr. Wardin Spurrit spent an extensive amount of time trying to learn more about this apparently one-of-a-kind specimen, claiming to have witnessed its silhouette multiple times while studying the Rift, but that it was near impossible to track due to its tendency of being able to "hop" between the endless alternate dimensions which encircle the Monster World. However, the effort was revitalized quite recently, and after a fortuitous encounter - the details of which we've been told shall remain murky, miry even - we've been able to study it and learn about its cryptic nature. It is assuredly highly intelligent, deeming itself "a Monster, of course" but one that is "too sophisticated to sing”, despite our campaigns of encouragement. It has stated that it prefers to be kept in dormancy inside this mechanism, so it can "ponder and think to itself." According to data collected vis-à-vis brainwaves, exactly what it mulls over 24/7 is "beyond your comprehension and none of your Humbugswax."
data.3145724444:
Designed by Daniel Negorev

Despite its eerie appearance, the Odd-o-Mobile outshines most other nearby objects by dangling beautiful baubles from its finger-like appendages. It spends most of its time burrowing underground, only popping up whenever Monsters are struggling to fall asleep and need to catch some Z's. Once surfaced, its colorful bric-o-brac helps lull them into a comfortable sleep. The Odd-o-Mobile prefers its subterranean dwellings since the sun would damage its light-sensitive skin - though it enjoys the company of Monsters above ground enough that it appears a little faded.
data.3016685881: Look! You can place an additional Mini-Mine on your islands now!... wait... this is just a recreation of the cardboard constructible standee from My Singing Monsters: The Board Game? And there's a Furcorn popping out, recalling part of the scene from the accompanying art print? Well... alright then.
data.3186652782: This commemorative decoration celebrates the first year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the rediscovery of Ethereal Island at the year's end.
data.996410560: This commemorative decoration celebrates the second year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the announcement of Rare Monsters at the year's end.
data.4030256997: This commemorative decoration celebrates the third year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the significance of Kayna's liberation from amber at the year's end.
data.3980257245: This commemorative decoration celebrates the fourth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the incursion of the Mirror Islands' alternate timeline at the year's end.
data.643904632: This commemorative decoration celebrates the fifth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the homecoming of the Supernatural Wubbox on Wublin Island at the year's end.
data.2700429014: This commemorative decoration celebrates the sixth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the unveiling of Epic Monsters at the year's end.
data.1806275955: This commemorative decoration celebrates the seventh year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the mind-blowing reveal of Psychic Island at the year's end.
data.2600413094: This commemorative decoration celebrates the eighth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the adventurous resettlement of the Colossingum at the year's end.
data.1369699331: This commemorative decoration celebrates the ninth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the revelation of Seasonal Shanty at the year's end.
data.3504057983: This commemorative decoration celebrates the tenth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the influx of special Mythical energies at the year's end.
data.461864410: This commemorative decoration celebrates the eleventh year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the Epic Wubbox's manifestation on Gold Island at the year's end.
data.2635272052: This commemorative decoration celebrates the twelfth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the emergence of the Ethereal Quint from the Synthesizer on Ethereal Workshop at the year's end.
data.1448074449: This commemorative decoration celebrates the thirteenth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the new and improved Friends Menu and Profile feature that allow players to express themselves better than ever at the year's end.
data.2698414071:
When Colossals are engaged in an act of creativity, they often describe the experience among themselves as being guided by a 'spirit of creation.' Depicted in ancient artworks as a kindly old being attuned to the power of nature, the spirit would whisper ideas and inspiration to its creator companion, and otherwise provide a backing track of gentle humming to which one could work. It was actually this piece of imagery that inspired the Colossals to imagine themselves as the guardians of the various islands of the Monster World, long before the cataclysm that ended the Dawn of Fire brought it about as reality.

Behind the scenes:
This Special Edition Decoration is based on one of the earliest pieces of concept art for My Singing Monsters, back when its project codename was Songster Island. The Monster-Handlers would like to extend a heartfelt thank-you for your support of My Singing Monsters! They couldn't do it without you!
data.419207500:
This Bawnzi is a scaled-down version of the Great Tree that resides in the Thumpieverse. Several artistic interpretations of this arboreal landmark have existed throughout the years, with this latest iteration understood to be the most accurate representation. The sigils of the various Thumpie songs festoon the length of its meandering trunk, and there's even a replica of the little wooden door through which Thumpies access its hollow interior. It's thanks to the Magic of the Trees that binds the various dimensions of the Stablo Mjehurica, the World Tree, that contact between the Monster World and the Thumpieverse is even possible in the first place.

The Monster-Handlers would like to extend a heartfelt thank-you for playing My Singing Monsters Thumpies, and congratulations for completing the whole game!
data.1677720271: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of Waveform! Outside of its duties as Autotuna, the T-Pirainha is an avid collector of artifacts and sunken treasure, some dating all the way back to the era of the Dawn of Fire!
data.3849029729: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of Waveform! Initially created as visualizers for the sonic stability of Clubbox concerts, these floating geometric forms are cool enough to serve as entertainment in their own right!
data.775379908: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of Waveform! If there's one thing that can be said about T-Pirainha and its cronies, it's that that their fashion sense is beyond compare - and these holograms are (not-quite-solid) proof!
data.1921159350: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of the Pomily Family Band! Each member of the Pomily is a star in its own right, but it's so much fun to see them all perform together!
data.4095140376: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of the Pomily Family Band! Flowers are the perfect expression of nature's beauty; for the Pomily, they also represent the power of peace and working together!
data.1061827005: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of the Pomily Family Band! What better way to recognize the unique relationship between the Pomily and the different foods they eat than a multicolored monument to munchies?
data.3413723998: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of BlitherPhish! Even if your Act is all about being tough and edgy, don't forget to be kind and appreciative of your adoring fans!
data.1307392496: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of BlitherPhish! These impressive contraptions rival the technological might of the Clubbox itself - could they be related somehow?
